📘 Causal Autoimmune Architecture Platform RuleSpeak

Rulebook for inferring the complete causal architecture of heterogeneous autoimmune disease from multi-omic cohort data, producing falsifiable mechanisms and ancestry-equitable predictions with calibrated uncertainty.

Declarative business rules rendered from the rulebook — the single source of truth. Every statement below is the plain-language reading of a formula; nothing here is hand-authored prose.

Use the in the top-right corner to turn each part of the documentation on or off — RuleSpeak, English, the raw formula, the author's description, and the links to a rule's inputs and consumers. Your choices are remembered.

Autoimmune Diseas

Target heterogeneous autoimmune disease definitions tracked across development, aging, tissues, and disease stages.

● Disease Label Ground truth string

Ground truth. Written directly to the database (datatype string); not computed from anything else. Everything derived ultimately traces back to values like this one.

Human-readable disease name.

● Is Complex Disease Ground truth boolean

Ground truth. Written directly to the database (datatype boolean); not computed from anything else. Everything derived ultimately traces back to values like this one.

True when the disease is a multifactorial complex disease beyond simple monogenic traits.

ƒ Name Calculated 1 hop from ground truth string DR-1
In RuleSpeak

An autoimmune diseas's name is computed as the disease label.

In English

Disease label.

As a formula
= Disease Label
Inputs 1 fields this rule reads
●Disease LabelAutoimmuneDiseases Human-readable disease name.
Ground truth at the leaves 1 the raw values this ultimately depends on
● Disease Label AutoimmuneDiseases

Display label for the autoimmune disease.

ƒ Relative Path Calculated 1 hop from ground truth string DR-2
In RuleSpeak

An autoimmune diseas's relative path is computed as the literal “/diseases/”, followed by the autoimmune disease ID.

In English

"/diseases/" joined with autoimmune disease id.

As a formula
= "/diseases/" & Autoimmune Disease ID
Inputs 1 fields this rule reads
●Autoimmune Disease IDAutoimmuneDiseases Stable identifier for each autoimmune disease (e.g. sle, ra, t1d).
Consumers 1 direct +1 transitive rules that read this one
↗ Parent Path DiseaseStages Relative Path DiseaseStages
Ground truth at the leaves 1 the raw values this ultimately depends on
● Autoimmune Disease ID AutoimmuneDiseases

Canonical path to this AutoimmuneDisease page: /diseases/<slug-or-id>.

Σ Count of Disease Stages Aggregation 1 hop from ground truth integer DR-3
In RuleSpeak

An autoimmune diseas's count of disease stages is the number of disease stages related to the autoimmune diseas.

In English

Count of DiseaseStages whose autoimmune disease is autoimmune disease id.

As a formula
= COUNTIFS(Autoimmune Disease, Autoimmune Disease ID)
Inputs 2 fields this rule reads
●Autoimmune DiseaseDiseaseStages Parent autoimmune disease. ●Autoimmune Disease IDAutoimmuneDiseases Stable identifier for each autoimmune disease (e.g. sle, ra, t1d).
Ground truth at the leaves 2 the raw values this ultimately depends on
● Autoimmune Disease DiseaseStages ● Autoimmune Disease ID AutoimmuneDiseases

Number of disease stages defined for this autoimmune disease.

Σ Count of Intervention Targets Aggregation 1 hop from ground truth integer DR-4
In RuleSpeak

An autoimmune diseas's count of intervention targets is the number of intervention targets related to the autoimmune diseas.

In English

Count of InterventionTargets whose autoimmune disease is autoimmune disease id.

As a formula
= COUNTIFS(Autoimmune Disease, Autoimmune Disease ID)
Inputs 2 fields this rule reads
●Autoimmune DiseaseInterventionTargets Target autoimmune disease. ●Autoimmune Disease IDAutoimmuneDiseases Stable identifier for each autoimmune disease (e.g. sle, ra, t1d).
Ground truth at the leaves 2 the raw values this ultimately depends on
● Autoimmune Disease InterventionTargets ● Autoimmune Disease ID AutoimmuneDiseases

Count of validated intervention targets linked to this disease.

Disease Stage

Ordered disease stages capturing presymptomatic, active, remission, and treatment-refractory phases along disease progression.

● Stage Label Ground truth string

Ground truth. Written directly to the database (datatype string); not computed from anything else. Everything derived ultimately traces back to values like this one.

Human-readable stage label (e.g. presymptomatic, active).

● Sort Order Ground truth integer

Ground truth. Written directly to the database (datatype integer); not computed from anything else. Everything derived ultimately traces back to values like this one.

Numeric ordering of stages along disease progression.

● Autoimmune Disease Ground truth string

Ground truth. Written directly to the database (datatype string); not computed from anything else. Everything derived ultimately traces back to values like this one.

Parent autoimmune disease.

ƒ Name Calculated 2 hops from ground truth string DR-5
In RuleSpeak

A disease stage's name is computed as the autoimmune disease disease label, followed by the literal “ — ”, followed by the stage label.

In English

(autoimmune disease disease label joined with " — ") joined with stage label.

As a formula
= Autoimmune Disease Disease Label & " — " & Stage Label
Inputs 2 fields this rule reads
ƒAutoimmune Disease Disease LabelDiseaseStages The disease label of the linked autoimmune disease. ●Stage LabelDiseaseStages Human-readable stage label (e.g. presymptomatic, active).
Ground truth at the leaves 4 the raw values this ultimately depends on
● Disease Label AutoimmuneDiseases ● Autoimmune Disease DiseaseStages ● Autoimmune Disease ID AutoimmuneDiseases ● Stage Label DiseaseStages

Display label combining disease and stage.

↗ Parent Path Lookup 2 hops from ground truth string DR-6
In RuleSpeak

A disease stage's parent path is the relative path of the disease stage's autoimmune disease.

In English

The relative path of the linked autoimmune disease.

As a formula
= INDEX(Relative Path, MATCH(Autoimmune Disease, Autoimmune Disease ID, 0))
Inputs 3 fields this rule reads
ƒRelative PathAutoimmuneDiseases "/diseases/" joined with autoimmune disease id. ●Autoimmune DiseaseDiseaseStages Parent autoimmune disease. ●Autoimmune Disease IDAutoimmuneDiseases Stable identifier for each autoimmune disease (e.g. sle, ra, t1d).
Consumers 1 direct rules that read this one
ƒ Relative Path DiseaseStages
Ground truth at the leaves 2 the raw values this ultimately depends on
● Autoimmune Disease ID AutoimmuneDiseases ● Autoimmune Disease DiseaseStages

Lookup: AutoimmuneDiseases.RelativePath via AutoimmuneDisease — used to chain this entity's path under its parent.

ƒ Relative Path Calculated 3 hops from ground truth string DR-7
In RuleSpeak

A disease stage's relative path is computed as the parent path, followed by the literal “/stages/”, followed by the disease stage ID.

In English

(parent path joined with "/stages/") joined with disease stage id.

As a formula
= Parent Path & "/stages/" & Disease Stage ID
Inputs 2 fields this rule reads
↗Parent PathDiseaseStages The relative path of the linked autoimmune disease. ●Disease Stage IDDiseaseStages Stable identifier for each disease stage.
Ground truth at the leaves 3 the raw values this ultimately depends on
● Autoimmune Disease ID AutoimmuneDiseases ● Autoimmune Disease DiseaseStages ● Disease Stage ID DiseaseStages

Path to this DiseaseStage page, chained under its AutoimmuneDisease parent.

ƒ Autoimmune Disease Disease Label Calculated 1 hop from ground truth string DR-8
In RuleSpeak

A disease stage's autoimmune disease disease label is the disease label of the disease stage's autoimmune disease.

In English

The disease label of the linked autoimmune disease.

As a formula
= INDEX(Disease Label, MATCH(Autoimmune Disease, Autoimmune Disease ID, 0))
Inputs 3 fields this rule reads
●Disease LabelAutoimmuneDiseases Human-readable disease name. ●Autoimmune DiseaseDiseaseStages Parent autoimmune disease. ●Autoimmune Disease IDAutoimmuneDiseases Stable identifier for each autoimmune disease (e.g. sle, ra, t1d).
Consumers 1 direct rules that read this one
ƒ Name DiseaseStages
Ground truth at the leaves 3 the raw values this ultimately depends on
● Disease Label AutoimmuneDiseases ● Autoimmune Disease DiseaseStages ● Autoimmune Disease ID AutoimmuneDiseases

Lookup of parent disease label.

ƒ Is Presymptomatic Calculated 1 hop from ground truth boolean DR-9
In RuleSpeak

The disease stage's is presymptomatic is determined by the following priority:

  1. true if the stage label is the literal “Presymptomatic”
  2. false otherwise
In English

If stage label is "Presymptomatic" then true(), otherwise false().

As a formula
= IF(Stage Label = "Presymptomatic", TRUE(), FALSE())
Inputs 1 fields this rule reads
●Stage LabelDiseaseStages Human-readable stage label (e.g. presymptomatic, active).
Ground truth at the leaves 1 the raw values this ultimately depends on
● Stage Label DiseaseStages

True when stage is presymptomatic diagnosis window.

Ground truth. Written directly to the database (datatype string); not computed from anything else. Everything derived ultimately traces back to values like this one.

As a formula
= Tissue Label

Count of OmicsAssays whose tissue is tissue id.

As a formula

Inputs 2 fields this rule reads

Number of omics assays performed on this tissue.

Omics Modality

Registry of omics assay modalities: RNA-seq, ATAC-seq, proteomics, metabolomics, methylomes, chromatin-conformation, immune-receptor repertoires, microbiomes, and long-read genomes.

● Modality Label Ground truth string

Ground truth. Written directly to the database (datatype string); not computed from anything else. Everything derived ultimately traces back to values like this one.

Human-readable modality name.

● Is Single Cell Ground truth boolean

Ground truth. Written directly to the database (datatype boolean); not computed from anything else. Everything derived ultimately traces back to values like this one.

True for single-cell or spatial assays resolving cellular states.

Federated Dataset

Ground truth. Written directly to the database (datatype string); not computed from anything else. Everything derived ultimately traces back to values like this one.

Geographic region; shifting environments may alter exposure profiles.

Ground truth. Written directly to the database (datatype boolean); not computed from anything else. Everything derived ultimately traces back to values like this one.

True when node uses privacy-preserving federated learning.

Variant Type

Classification of genomic variant mechanisms: regulatory, coding, repeat expansions, mobile-element insertions, HLA haplotypes, structural variants, de novo mutations, somatic mosaicism, mitochondrial variation.

Ground truth. Written directly to the database (datatype string); not computed from anything else. Everything derived ultimately traces back to values like this one.

Ground truth. Written directly to the database (datatype boolean); not computed from anything else. Everything derived ultimately traces back to values like this one.

Individual

Ancestrally diverse cohort participants whose phased genomes, omics profiles, exposures, and clinical phenotypes feed causal architecture inference.

● Given Name Ground truth string

Ground truth. Written directly to the database (datatype string); not computed from anything else. Everything derived ultimately traces back to values like this one.

Participant given name (de-identified mock).

● Family Name Ground truth string

Ground truth. Written directly to the database (datatype string); not computed from anything else. Everything derived ultimately traces back to values like this one.

Participant family name (de-identified mock).

● Ancestry Label Ground truth string

Ground truth. Written directly to the database (datatype string); not computed from anything else. Everything derived ultimately traces back to values like this one.

Ancestry label for population stratification and ancestry-equitable risk prediction.

● Age Years Ground truth integer

Ground truth. Written directly to the database (datatype integer); not computed from anything else. Everything derived ultimately traces back to values like this one.

Age in years spanning development and aging windows.

● Is Ancestry Absent From Training Ground truth boolean

Ground truth. Written directly to the database (datatype boolean); not computed from anything else. Everything derived ultimately traces back to values like this one.

True when individual ancestry was absent from primary training data.

● Federated Dataset Ground truth string

Ground truth. Written directly to the database (datatype string); not computed from anything else. Everything derived ultimately traces back to values like this one.

Federated dataset node when enrolled via privacy-preserving federation.

● Enrollment Date Ground truth datetime

Ground truth. Written directly to the database (datatype datetime); not computed from anything else. Everything derived ultimately traces back to values like this one.

Longitudinal cohort enrollment date.

● Has Cryptic Relatedness Flag Ground truth boolean

Ground truth. Written directly to the database (datatype boolean); not computed from anything else. Everything derived ultimately traces back to values like this one.

True when cryptic relatedness or assortative mating bias detected.

ƒ Name Calculated 1 hop from ground truth string DR-22
In RuleSpeak

An individual's name is computed as the given name, followed by a space, followed by the family name.

In English

(given name joined with " ") joined with family name.

As a formula
= Given Name & " " & Family Name
Inputs 2 fields this rule reads
●Given NameIndividuals Participant given name (de-identified mock). ●Family NameIndividuals Participant family name (de-identified mock).
Ground truth at the leaves 2 the raw values this ultimately depends on
● Given Name Individuals ● Family Name Individuals

Display name for the individual.

ƒ Slug Calculated 1 hop from ground truth string DR-23
In RuleSpeak ⚠︎ mechanical

An individual's slug is computed as the lower-cased family name, followed by a hyphen, followed by the given name with every a space replaced by a hyphen.

In English

((family name joined with "-") joined with given name) lowercased with “ ” replaced by “-”.

As a formula
= SUBSTITUTE(LOWER(Family Name & "-" & Given Name), " ", "-")
Inputs 2 fields this rule reads
●Family NameIndividuals Participant family name (de-identified mock). ●Given NameIndividuals Participant given name (de-identified mock).
Consumers 1 direct +28 transitive rules that read this one

Ground truth at the leaves 2 the raw values this ultimately depends on
● Family Name Individuals ● Given Name Individuals

ƒ Federated Dataset Node Label Calculated 1 hop from ground truth string DR-25
In RuleSpeak

The individual's federated dataset node label is determined by the following priority:

  1. an empty string if the federated dataset is blank
  2. the node label of the individual's federated dataset otherwise
In English

If federated dataset is "" then "", otherwise the node label of the linked federated dataset.

As a formula
= IF(Federated Dataset = "", "", INDEX(Node Label, MATCH(Federated Dataset, Federated Dataset ID, 0)))
Inputs 3 fields this rule reads
●Federated DatasetIndividuals Federated dataset node when enrolled via privacy-preserving federation. ●Node LabelFederatedDatasets Human-readable federated node name. ●Federated Dataset IDFederatedDatasets Stable identifier for each federated data node.
Ground truth at the leaves 3 the raw values this ultimately depends on
● Federated Dataset Individuals ● Node Label FederatedDatasets ● Federated Dataset ID FederatedDatasets

Lookup of federated node label.

As a formula

● Individual EpistaticInteractions ● Individual ID Individuals

ƒ Rare Variant Burden Score Calculated 2 hops from ground truth number DR-29
In RuleSpeak

The individual's rare variant burden score is determined by the following priority:

  1. the count of genomic variants divided by the age years if the age years is greater than 0
  2. 0 otherwise
In English

If age years is greater than 0 then (count of genomic variants divided by age years), otherwise 0.

As a formula
= IF(Age Years > 0, Count of Genomic Variants / Age Years, 0)
Inputs 2 fields this rule reads
●Age YearsIndividuals Age in years spanning development and aging windows. ΣCount of Genomic VariantsIndividuals Count of GenomicVariants whose individual is individual id.
Consumers 1 direct rules that read this one
ƒ Causal Architecture Score Individuals
Ground truth at the leaves 3 the raw values this ultimately depends on
● Age Years Individuals ● Individual GenomicVariants ● Individual ID Individuals

2nd-order score from rare variant count normalized by age.

ƒ Causal Architecture Score Calculated 3 hops from ground truth number DR-30
In RuleSpeak

An individual's causal architecture score is computed as the count of causal mechanisms times 10 plus the count of epistatic interactions times 5 plus the rare variant burden score.

In English

((count of causal mechanisms times 10) plus (count of epistatic interactions times 5)) plus rare variant burden score.

As a formula
= Count of Causal Mechanisms * 10 + Count of Epistatic Interactions * 5 + Rare Variant Burden Score
Inputs 3 fields this rule reads
ΣCount of Causal MechanismsIndividuals Count of CausalMechanisms whose individual is individual id. ΣCount of Epistatic InteractionsIndividuals Count of EpistaticInteractions whose individual is individual id. ƒRare Variant Burden ScoreIndividuals If age years is greater than 0 then (count of genomic variants divided by age years), otherwise 0.
Ground truth at the leaves 5 the raw values this ultimately depends on
● Individual CausalMechanisms ● Individual ID Individuals ● Individual EpistaticInteractions ● Age Years Individuals ● Individual GenomicVariants

3rd-order composite of causal mechanisms and epistasis density.

ƒ Is Development Window Calculated 1 hop from ground truth boolean DR-31
In RuleSpeak

The individual's is development window is determined by the following priority:

  1. true if the age years is at most 25
  2. false otherwise
In English

If age years is at most 25 then true(), otherwise false().

As a formula
= IF(Age Years <= 25, TRUE(), FALSE())
Inputs 1 fields this rule reads
●Age YearsIndividuals Age in years spanning development and aging windows.
Ground truth at the leaves 1 the raw values this ultimately depends on
● Age Years Individuals

True when age falls in developmental effects window (0-25).

ƒ Is Aging Window Calculated 1 hop from ground truth boolean DR-32
In RuleSpeak

The individual's is aging window is determined by the following priority:

  1. true if the age years is at least 60
  2. false otherwise
In English

If age years is at least 60 then true(), otherwise false().

As a formula
= IF(Age Years >= 60, TRUE(), FALSE())
Inputs 1 fields this rule reads
●Age YearsIndividuals Age in years spanning development and aging windows.
Ground truth at the leaves 1 the raw values this ultimately depends on
● Age Years Individuals

True when age falls in aging window (60+).

An individual's count confirmed causal nodes is the number of the individual's causal mechanisms that are causal architecture nodes.

Count of CausalMechanisms whose individual is individual id and is causal architecture node is true().

As a formula
= COUNTIFS(Individual, Individual ID, Is Causal Architecture Node, TRUE())

Σ Sum Confirmed Causal Confidence Aggregation 8 hops from ground truth number DR-34
In RuleSpeak

An individual's sum confirmed causal confidence is the total causal confidence across the individual's causal mechanisms that are causal architecture nodes.

In English

Sum of causal confidence across all CausalMechanisms whose individual is individual id and is causal architecture node is true().

As a formula
= SUMIFS(Causal Confidence, Individual, Individual ID, Is Causal Architecture Node, TRUE())
Inputs 4 fields this rule reads
ƒCausal ConfidenceCausalMechanisms If ((((0.30 times if count qualified evidence is at least 4 then 1, otherwise (count qualified evidence divided by 4)) plus (0.20 times if count modalities supporting is at least 3 then 1, otherwise (count modalities supporting divided by 3))) plus (0.30 times replication fraction)) plus (0.20 times if survives negative controls then 1, otherwise 0)) is greater than 1 then 1, otherwise ((((0.30 times if count qualified evidence is at least 4 then 1, otherwise (count qualified evidence divided by 4)) plus (0.20 times if count modalities supporting is at least 3 then 1, otherwise (count modalities supporting divided by 3))) plus (0.30 times replication fraction)) plus (0.20 times if survives negative controls then 1, otherwise 0)). ●IndividualCausalMechanisms Individual for whom mechanism is inferred. ●Individual IDIndividuals Stable identifier for each individual in the million-person cohort. ƒIs Causal Architecture NodeCausalMechanisms If causal confidence is at least 0.7, is experimentally falsifiable, not is spurious derived, and variant is causal candidate or environmental exposure is not "" then true(), otherwise false().

⇢ Genomic Variants Relationship string

A pointer to Genomic Variant. It is not derived from anything else — it is the link the rest of the DAG hangs off.

Genomic variants for this individual.

⇢ Omics Assays Relationship string

A pointer to Omics Assay. It is not derived from anything else — it is the link the rest of the DAG hangs off.

Omics assays for this individual.

⇢ Environmental Exposures Relationship string

A pointer to Environmental Exposure. It is not derived from anything else — it is the link the rest of the DAG hangs off.

Longitudinal environmental exposures.

⇢ Treatments Relationship string

A pointer to Treatment. It is not derived from anything else — it is the link the rest of the DAG hangs off.

Treatment histories.

⇢ Clinical Phenotypes Relationship string

A pointer to Clinical Phenotype. It is not derived from anything else — it is the link the rest of the DAG hangs off.

Clinical phenotypes.

⇢ Causal Mechanisms Relationship string

A pointer to Causal Mechanism. It is not derived from anything else — it is the link the rest of the DAG hangs off.

Inferred causal mechanisms.

⇢ Epistatic Interactions Relationship string

A pointer to Epistatic Interaction. It is not derived from anything else — it is the link the rest of the DAG hangs off.

Epistatic interactions.

⇢ Counterfactual Trajectories Relationship string

A pointer to Counterfactual Trajectory. It is not derived from anything else — it is the link the rest of the DAG hangs off.

Counterfactual trajectories.

⇢ Individual Predictions Relationship string

A pointer to Individual Prediction. It is not derived from anything else — it is the link the rest of the DAG hangs off.

Individual predictions.

● Case Narrative Ground truth string

Ground truth. Written directly to the database (datatype string); not computed from anything else. Everything derived ultimately traces back to values like this one.

PANEL 1 of the witness (raw leaf, read by nothing downstream): the natural-language intake case the LLM was handed. Every raw observation below is extracted from THIS text; each leaf's SourceQuote points back into it so the extraction is human-checkable independently of the verdict. Synthetic but transparent — invented case, literature-aligned loci/thresholds.

Σ Count Serology Panels Aggregation 1 hop from ground truth number DR-41
In RuleSpeak

An individual's count serology panels is the number of serology observations related to the individual.

In English

Count of SerologyObservations whose individual is individual id.

As a formula
= COUNTIFS(Individual, Individual ID)
Inputs 2 fields this rule reads
⇢IndividualSerologyObservations FK -> Individuals.IndividualId. ●Individual IDIndividuals Stable identifier for each individual in the million-person cohort.
Ground truth at the leaves 2 the raw values this ultimately depends on
⇢ Individual SerologyObservations ● Individual ID Individuals

Number of serology panels for this individual.

Σ Count Pre Nephritic Signature Panels Aggregation 4 hops from ground truth number DR-42
In RuleSpeak

An individual's count pre nephritic signature panels is the number of the individual's serology observations that are pre nephritic signature panels.

In English

Count of SerologyObservations whose individual is individual id and is pre nephritic signature panel is true().

As a formula
= COUNTIFS(Individual, Individual ID, Is Pre Nephritic Signature Panel, TRUE())
Inputs 3 fields this rule reads
⇢IndividualSerologyObservations FK -> Individuals.IndividualId. ●Individual IDIndividuals Stable identifier for each individual in the million-person cohort. ƒIs Pre Nephritic Signature PanelSerologyObservations If anti ds dna trend is "Rising" and complement trend is "Falling" then true(), otherwise false().
Consumers 2 direct rules that read this one
ƒ Is in Pre Nephritic Signature Cluster Individuals ƒ Signature Strength Individuals
Ground truth at the leaves 7 the raw values this ultimately depends on
⇢ Individual SerologyObservations ● Individual ID Individuals ● Anti Ds Dna IU SerologyObservations ⇢ Prior Observation SerologyObservations ● Serology Observation ID SerologyObservations ● Complement C3 SerologyObservations ● Complement C4 SerologyObservations

How many of this individual's panels exhibit the pre-nephritic serology signature (rising anti-dsDNA + falling complement before overt nephritis). The corpus-level roll-up of the per-panel signal; emergent from the raw series.

ƒ Is in Pre Nephritic Signature Cluster Calculated 5 hops from ground truth boolean DR-43
In RuleSpeak

The individual's is in pre nephritic signature cluster is determined by the following priority:

  1. true if the count pre nephritic signature panels is at least 1
  2. false otherwise
In English

If count pre nephritic signature panels is at least 1 then true(), otherwise false().

As a formula
= IF(Count Pre Nephritic Signature Panels>=1,TRUE(),FALSE())
Inputs 1 fields this rule reads
ΣCount Pre Nephritic Signature PanelsIndividuals Count of SerologyObservations whose individual is individual id and is pre nephritic signature panel is true().
Ground truth at the leaves 7 the raw values this ultimately depends on
⇢ Individual SerologyObservations ● Individual ID Individuals ● Anti Ds Dna IU SerologyObservations ⇢ Prior Observation SerologyObservations ● Serology Observation ID SerologyObservations ● Complement C3 SerologyObservations ● Complement C4 SerologyObservations

DERIVED cluster membership: TRUE when this individual's own raw serology series ever showed the pre-nephritic signature. The discovery 'this serology signature precedes nephritis' becomes a reproducible, witnessed corpus-level cluster — not a label anyone assigned.

ƒ Signature Strength Calculated 5 hops from ground truth number DR-44
In RuleSpeak

The individual's signature strength is determined by the following priority:

  1. 2 if the count pre nephritic signature panels is at least 2
  2. 1 if the count pre nephritic signature panels is at least 1
  3. 0 otherwise
In English

If count pre nephritic signature panels is at least 2 then 2, otherwise if count pre nephritic signature panels is at least 1 then 1, otherwise 0.

As a formula
= IF(Count Pre Nephritic Signature Panels>=2,2,IF(Count Pre Nephritic Signature Panels>=1,1,0))
Inputs 1 fields this rule reads
ΣCount Pre Nephritic Signature PanelsIndividuals Count of SerologyObservations whose individual is individual id and is pre nephritic signature panel is true().
Ground truth at the leaves 7 the raw values this ultimately depends on
⇢ Individual SerologyObservations ● Individual ID Individuals ● Anti Ds Dna IU SerologyObservations ⇢ Prior Observation SerologyObservations ● Serology Observation ID SerologyObservations ● Complement C3 SerologyObservations ● Complement C4 SerologyObservations

DERIVED 0/1/2 strength of the pre-nephritic signature: 2 when it appears on >=2 panels (persistent), 1 on exactly one panel, 0 when absent. Drives the emphasis of cluster members on the cohort scatter.

Σ Max Progression State Order Aggregation 5 hops from ground truth number DR-45
In RuleSpeak

An individual's max progression state order is the largest progression state order across the serology observations related to the individual.

In English

Largest progression state order across all SerologyObservations whose individual is individual id.

As a formula
= MAXIFS(Progression State Order, Individual, Individual ID)
Inputs 3 fields this rule reads
ƒProgression State OrderSerologyObservations If progression state key is "BiopsyIndicated" then 5, otherwise if progression state key is "RenalFlareRisk" then 4, otherwise if progression state key is "EarlyNephritis" then 3, otherwise if progression state key is "SerologicActive" then 2, otherwise 1. ⇢IndividualSerologyObservations FK -> Individuals.IndividualId. ●Individual IDIndividuals Stable identifier for each individual in the million-person cohort.
Consumers 1 direct +8 transitive rules that read this one
ƒ Nephritis Progression State Key Individuals Is Disease Progressing Individuals Current Progression State ID Individuals Individual Progression State Key IndividualPredictions Individual is Disease Progressing IndividualPredictions Reachable States Ahead Individuals Recommended Treatment Line IndividualPredictions Treatment Line Deciding Factor IndividualPredictions Progression Vs Actionability Disagree IndividualPredictions
Ground truth at the leaves 9 the raw values this ultimately depends on
● Proteinuria G Per Day SerologyObservations ● Has Active Urinary Sediment SerologyObservations ● Anti Ds Dna IU SerologyObservations ⇢ Prior Observation SerologyObservations ● Serology Observation ID SerologyObservations ● Complement C3 SerologyObservations ● Complement C4 SerologyObservations ⇢ Individual SerologyObservations ● Individual ID Individuals

Highest progression-state order reached across this individual's serology panels (worst/current state).

Σ Latest Sledai Score Aggregation 5 hops from ground truth number DR-46
In RuleSpeak

An individual's latest sledai score is the largest sledai score across the serology observations related to the individual.

In English

Largest sledai score across all SerologyObservations whose individual is individual id.

As a formula
= MAXIFS(Sledai Score, Individual, Individual ID)
Inputs 3 fields this rule reads
ƒSledai ScoreSerologyObservations Sledai renal points plus sledai serology points. ⇢IndividualSerologyObservations FK -> Individuals.IndividualId. ●Individual IDIndividuals Stable identifier for each individual in the million-person cohort.
Consumers 2 direct rules that read this one
ƒ Activity Tier Individuals ƒ Is High Disease Activity Individuals
Ground truth at the leaves 9 the raw values this ultimately depends on
● Proteinuria G Per Day SerologyObservations ● Has Active Urinary Sediment SerologyObservations ● Complement C3 SerologyObservations ⇢ Prior Observation SerologyObservations ● Serology Observation ID SerologyObservations ● Complement C4 SerologyObservations ● Anti Ds Dna IU SerologyObservations ⇢ Individual SerologyObservations ● Individual ID Individuals

Peak SLEDAI activity score across this individual's panels (derived).

ƒ Nephritis Progression State Key Calculated 6 hops from ground truth string DR-47
In RuleSpeak

The individual's nephritis progression state key is determined by the following priority:

  1. the literal “BiopsyIndicated” if the max progression state order is at least 5
  2. the literal “RenalFlareRisk” if the max progression state order is at least 4
  3. the literal “EarlyNephritis” if the max progression state order is at least 3
  4. the literal “SerologicActive” if the max progression state order is at least 2
  5. the literal “PresymptomaticAutoimmunity” otherwise
In English

If max progression state order is at least 5 then "BiopsyIndicated", otherwise if max progression state order is at least 4 then "RenalFlareRisk", otherwise if max progression state order is at least 3 then "EarlyNephritis", otherwise if max progression state order is at least 2 then "SerologicActive", otherwise "PresymptomaticAutoimmunity".

As a formula
= IF(Max Progression State Order>=5,"BiopsyIndicated",IF(Max Progression State Order>=4,"RenalFlareRisk",IF(Max Progression State Order>=3,"EarlyNephritis",IF(Max Progression State Order>=2,"SerologicActive","PresymptomaticAutoimmunity"))))
Inputs 1 fields this rule reads
ΣMax Progression State OrderIndividuals Largest progression state order across all SerologyObservations whose individual is individual id.
Consumers 3 direct +5 transitive rules that read this one
ƒ Is Disease Progressing Individuals ƒ Current Progression State ID Individuals ↗ Individual Progression State Key IndividualPredictions Individual is Disease Progressing IndividualPredictions Reachable States Ahead Individuals Recommended Treatment Line IndividualPredictions Treatment Line Deciding Factor IndividualPredictions Progression Vs Actionability Disagree IndividualPredictions
Ground truth at the leaves 9 the raw values this ultimately depends on
● Proteinuria G Per Day SerologyObservations ● Has Active Urinary Sediment SerologyObservations ● Anti Ds Dna IU SerologyObservations ⇢ Prior Observation SerologyObservations ● Serology Observation ID SerologyObservations ● Complement C3 SerologyObservations ● Complement C4 SerologyObservations ⇢ Individual SerologyObservations ● Individual ID Individuals

DERIVED current disease state for the lupus-nephritis-progression machine: decoded from the highest state order reached. The subject- state column of the state machine; never hand-set.

ƒ Activity Tier Calculated 6 hops from ground truth string DR-48
In RuleSpeak

The individual's activity tier is determined by the following priority:

  1. the literal “High / flare” if the latest sledai score is at least 12
  2. the literal “Moderate” if the latest sledai score is at least 6
  3. the literal “Mild” if the latest sledai score is at least 1
  4. the literal “Quiescent” otherwise
In English

If latest sledai score is at least 12 then "High / flare", otherwise if latest sledai score is at least 6 then "Moderate", otherwise if latest sledai score is at least 1 then "Mild", otherwise "Quiescent".

As a formula
= IF(Latest Sledai Score>=12,"High / flare",IF(Latest Sledai Score>=6,"Moderate",IF(Latest Sledai Score>=1,"Mild","Quiescent")))
Inputs 1 fields this rule reads
ΣLatest Sledai ScoreIndividuals Largest sledai score across all SerologyObservations whose individual is individual id.
Ground truth at the leaves 9 the raw values this ultimately depends on
● Proteinuria G Per Day SerologyObservations ● Has Active Urinary Sediment SerologyObservations ● Complement C3 SerologyObservations ⇢ Prior Observation SerologyObservations ● Serology Observation ID SerologyObservations ● Complement C4 SerologyObservations ● Anti Ds Dna IU SerologyObservations ⇢ Individual SerologyObservations ● Individual ID Individuals

Derived disease-activity tier from peak SLEDAI score.

ƒ Is High Disease Activity Calculated 6 hops from ground truth boolean DR-49
In RuleSpeak

The individual's is high disease activity is determined by the following priority:

  1. true if the latest sledai score is at least 12
  2. false otherwise
In English

If latest sledai score is at least 12 then true(), otherwise false().

As a formula
= IF(Latest Sledai Score>=12,TRUE(),FALSE())
Inputs 1 fields this rule reads
ΣLatest Sledai ScoreIndividuals Largest sledai score across all SerologyObservations whose individual is individual id.
Ground truth at the leaves 9 the raw values this ultimately depends on
● Proteinuria G Per Day SerologyObservations ● Has Active Urinary Sediment SerologyObservations ● Complement C3 SerologyObservations ⇢ Prior Observation SerologyObservations ● Serology Observation ID SerologyObservations ● Complement C4 SerologyObservations ● Anti Ds Dna IU SerologyObservations ⇢ Individual SerologyObservations ● Individual ID Individuals

TRUE when peak SLEDAI >= 12.

ƒ Is Disease Progressing Calculated 7 hops from ground truth boolean DR-50
In RuleSpeak

The individual's is disease progressing is determined by the following priority:

  1. true if any of these hold:
    • the nephritis progression state key is the literal “EarlyNephritis”
    • the nephritis progression state key is the literal “RenalFlareRisk”
    • the nephritis progression state key is the literal “BiopsyIndicated”
  2. false otherwise
In English

If nephritis progression state key is "EarlyNephritis", nephritis progression state key is "RenalFlareRisk", or nephritis progression state key is "BiopsyIndicated" then true(), otherwise false().

As a formula
= IF(OR(Nephritis Progression State Key="EarlyNephritis",Nephritis Progression State Key="RenalFlareRisk",Nephritis Progression State Key="BiopsyIndicated"),TRUE(),FALSE())
Inputs 1 fields this rule reads
ƒNephritis Progression State KeyIndividuals If max progression state order is at least 5 then "BiopsyIndicated", otherwise if max progression state order is at least 4 then "RenalFlareRisk", otherwise if max progression state order is at least 3 then "EarlyNephritis", otherwise if max progression state order is at least 2 then "SerologicActive", otherwise "PresymptomaticAutoimmunity".
Consumers 1 direct +1 transitive rules that read this one
↗ Individual is Disease Progressing IndividualPredictions Progression Vs Actionability Disagree IndividualPredictions
Ground truth at the leaves 9 the raw values this ultimately depends on
● Proteinuria G Per Day SerologyObservations ● Has Active Urinary Sediment SerologyObservations ● Anti Ds Dna IU SerologyObservations ⇢ Prior Observation SerologyObservations ● Serology Observation ID SerologyObservations ● Complement C3 SerologyObservations ● Complement C4 SerologyObservations ⇢ Individual SerologyObservations ● Individual ID Individuals

TRUE when the disease-state simulator places the patient in an active/worsening renal state (independent of the actionability gate).

Σ Target Pathway Code Aggregation 2 hops from ground truth number DR-51
In RuleSpeak

An individual's target pathway code is the largest target pathway code across the causal mechanisms related to the individual.

In English

Largest target pathway code across all CausalMechanisms whose individual is individual id.

As a formula
= MAXIFS(Target Pathway Code, Individual, Individual ID)
Inputs 3 fields this rule reads
ƒTarget Pathway CodeCausalMechanisms If target pathway is "type-I-IFN" then 1, otherwise if target pathway is "B-cell/autoantibody" then 2, otherwise if target pathway is "T-cell-costim" then 3, otherwise if target pathway is "IL-17/23" then 4, otherwise 0. ●IndividualCausalMechanisms Individual for whom mechanism is inferred. ●Individual IDIndividuals Stable identifier for each individual in the million-person cohort.
Consumers 1 direct +3 transitive rules that read this one
ƒ Target Pathway Individuals Individual Target Pathway IndividualPredictions Recommended Treatment Line IndividualPredictions Treatment Line Deciding Factor IndividualPredictions
Ground truth at the leaves 3 the raw values this ultimately depends on
● Target Pathway CausalMechanisms ● Individual CausalMechanisms ● Individual ID Individuals

Resolved pathway code from this individual's causal mechanism (MAXIFS).

ƒ Target Pathway Calculated 3 hops from ground truth string DR-52
In RuleSpeak

The individual's target pathway is determined by the following priority:

  1. the literal “type-I-IFN” if the target pathway code is 1
  2. the literal “B-cell/autoantibody” if the target pathway code is 2
  3. the literal “T-cell-costim” if the target pathway code is 3
  4. the literal “IL-17/23” if the target pathway code is 4
  5. an empty string otherwise
In English

If target pathway code equals 1 then "type-I-IFN", otherwise if target pathway code equals 2 then "B-cell/autoantibody", otherwise if target pathway code equals 3 then "T-cell-costim", otherwise if target pathway code equals 4 then "IL-17/23", otherwise "".

As a formula
= IF(Target Pathway Code=1,"type-I-IFN",IF(Target Pathway Code=2,"B-cell/autoantibody",IF(Target Pathway Code=3,"T-cell-costim",IF(Target Pathway Code=4,"IL-17/23",""))))
Inputs 1 fields this rule reads
ΣTarget Pathway CodeIndividuals Largest target pathway code across all CausalMechanisms whose individual is individual id.
Consumers 1 direct +2 transitive rules that read this one
↗ Individual Target Pathway IndividualPredictions Recommended Treatment Line IndividualPredictions Treatment Line Deciding Factor IndividualPredictions
Ground truth at the leaves 3 the raw values this ultimately depends on
● Target Pathway CausalMechanisms ● Individual CausalMechanisms ● Individual ID Individuals

Decoded druggable pathway implicated by this individual's mechanism.

↗ Reachable States Ahead Lookup 8 hops from ground truth integer DR-53
In RuleSpeak

An individual's reachable states ahead is the reachable state count of the individual's current progression state ID.

In English

The reachable state count of the linked current progression state id.

As a formula
= INDEX(Reachable State Count, MATCH(Current Progression State ID, Machine State ID, 0))
Inputs 3 fields this rule reads
ΣReachable State CountMachineStates Count of vw_state_transition_rules_closure whose from id is machine state id. ƒCurrent Progression State IDIndividuals "lupus-nephritis-progression--" joined with nephritis progression state key lowercased. ●Machine State IDMachineStates Slug PK, e.g. diagnosis-lifecycle--intake.
Ground truth at the leaves 10 the raw values this ultimately depends on
● Machine State ID MachineStates ● Proteinuria G Per Day SerologyObservations ● Has Active Urinary Sediment SerologyObservations ● Anti Ds Dna IU SerologyObservations ⇢ Prior Observation SerologyObservations ● Serology Observation ID SerologyObservations ● Complement C3 SerologyObservations ● Complement C4 SerologyObservations ⇢ Individual SerologyObservations ● Individual ID Individuals

How many disease states are still reachable ahead of THIS patient from their current state, via the transition closure (looks up MachineStates.ReachableStateCount for CurrentProgressionStateId). A closure-derived prognostic horizon: Diego at BiopsyIndicated has 0 ahead (terminal); a Presymptomatic patient has 5. NOT derivable from the severity rank alone (the count is non-monotonic in rank because the machine branches into remission).

Genomic Variant

Genomic variant calls per individual spanning regulatory, coding, structural, HLA haplotypes, de novo mutations, somatic mosaicism, and mitochondrial variation.

● Variant Label Ground truth string

Ground truth. Written directly to the database (datatype string); not computed from anything else. Everything derived ultimately traces back to values like this one.

Human-readable variant label (gene/locus).

● Individual Ground truth string

Ground truth. Written directly to the database (datatype string); not computed from anything else. Everything derived ultimately traces back to values like this one.

Individual carrying this variant.

● Variant Type Ground truth string

Ground truth. Written directly to the database (datatype string); not computed from anything else. Everything derived ultimately traces back to values like this one.

Variant mechanism classification.

● Allele Frequency Ground truth number

Ground truth. Written directly to the database (datatype number); not computed from anything else. Everything derived ultimately traces back to values like this one.

Population allele frequency for rare-variant burden.

● Has Allele Specific Expression Ground truth boolean

Ground truth. Written directly to the database (datatype boolean); not computed from anything else. Everything derived ultimately traces back to values like this one.

True when allele-specific expression observed.

ƒ Name Calculated 1 hop from ground truth string DR-54
In RuleSpeak

A genomic variant's name is computed as the variant label.

In English

Variant label.

As a formula
= Variant Label
Inputs 1 fields this rule reads
●Variant LabelGenomicVariants Human-readable variant label (gene/locus).
Ground truth at the leaves 1 the raw values this ultimately depends on
● Variant Label GenomicVariants

Display label.

↗ Parent Path Lookup 3 hops from ground truth string DR-55
In RuleSpeak

A genomic variant's parent path is the relative path of the genomic variant's individual.

In English

The relative path of the linked individual.

As a formula
= INDEX(Relative Path, MATCH(Individual, Individual ID, 0))
Inputs 3 fields this rule reads
ƒRelative PathIndividuals "/intake/new-patient/" joined with slug. ●IndividualGenomicVariants Individual carrying this variant. ●Individual IDIndividuals Stable identifier for each individual in the million-person cohort.
Consumers 1 direct rules that read this one
ƒ Relative Path GenomicVariants
Ground truth at the leaves 4 the raw values this ultimately depends on
● Family Name Individuals ● Given Name Individuals ● Individual GenomicVariants ● Individual ID Individuals

Lookup: Individuals.RelativePath via Individual — used to chain this entity's path under its parent.

ƒ Relative Path Calculated 4 hops from ground truth string DR-56
In RuleSpeak

A genomic variant's relative path is computed as the parent path, followed by the literal “/variants/”, followed by the genomic variant ID.

In English

(parent path joined with "/variants/") joined with genomic variant id.

As a formula
= Parent Path & "/variants/" & Genomic Variant ID
Inputs 2 fields this rule reads
↗Parent PathGenomicVariants The relative path of the linked individual. ●Genomic Variant IDGenomicVariants Stable identifier for each genomic variant record.
Ground truth at the leaves 5 the raw values this ultimately depends on
● Family Name Individuals ● Given Name Individuals ● Individual GenomicVariants ● Individual ID Individuals ● Genomic Variant ID GenomicVariants

Path to this GenomicVariant page, chained under its Individual parent.

ƒ Variant Type Label Calculated 1 hop from ground truth string DR-57
In RuleSpeak

A genomic variant's variant type label is the type label of the genomic variant's variant type.

In English

The type label of the linked variant type.

As a formula
= INDEX(Type Label, MATCH(Variant Type, Variant Type ID, 0))
Inputs 3 fields this rule reads
●Type LabelVariantTypes Human-readable variant mechanism label. ●Variant TypeGenomicVariants Variant mechanism classification. ●Variant Type IDVariantTypes Stable identifier for each variant type.
Ground truth at the leaves 3 the raw values this ultimately depends on
● Type Label VariantTypes ● Variant Type GenomicVariants ● Variant Type ID VariantTypes

Lookup of variant type label.

↗ Variant Class is Rare Lookup 1 hop from ground truth boolean DR-58
In RuleSpeak

A genomic variant's variant class is rare is true when the genomic variant's variant type is a rare variant class.

In English

The is rare variant class of the linked variant type.

As a formula
= INDEX(Is Rare Variant Class, MATCH(Variant Type, Variant Type ID, 0))
Inputs 3 fields this rule reads
●Is Rare Variant ClassVariantTypes True when this type contributes to rare-variant burden scoring. ●Variant TypeGenomicVariants Variant mechanism classification. ●Variant Type IDVariantTypes Stable identifier for each variant type.
Consumers 1 direct +34 transitive rules that read this one
ƒ Is Causal Candidate GenomicVariants Variant is Causal Candidate CausalMechanisms Is Causal Architecture Node CausalMechanisms Count Confirmed Causal Nodes Individuals Sum Confirmed Causal Confidence Individuals Is Mechanism Matched Treatments Is Ancestry Transportable CausalMechanisms Individual Confirmed Node Count IndividualPredictions Individual Causal Mass IndividualPredictions Is Treatment Response Predicted Treatments Treatment Response Deciding Factor Treatments Count Cross Ancestry Confirmed Nodes Individuals Predicted Value IndividualPredictions Rests on Confirmed Mechanism IndividualPredictions Is Falsifiability Backed IndividualPredictions Count Predicted Treatment Responses Individuals Individual Cross Ancestry Node Count IndividualPredictions Patient Stratification Tier IndividualPredictions Is Clinically Actionable IndividualPredictions Lifecycle State Key IndividualPredictions Has Spurious Correlation Flag IndividualPredictions Is Severity Actionable IndividualPredictions Severity Deciding Factor IndividualPredictions Treatment Response Deciding Factor IndividualPredictions Deciding Gate IndividualPredictions Recommended Treatment Line IndividualPredictions Treatment Line Deciding Factor IndividualPredictions Has Predicted Treatment Response Individuals Is Transportable to Absent Ancestry IndividualPredictions Progression Vs Actionability Disagree IndividualPredictions Is High Confidence Prediction IndividualPredictions Individual Has Predicted Treatment Response IndividualPredictions Is Ancestry Transport Safe IndividualPredictions Transport Gate Status IndividualPredictions Is Treatment Response Actionable IndividualPredictions

● Is Rare Variant Class VariantTypes ● Variant Type GenomicVariants ● Variant Type ID VariantTypes

Whether this variant's class is a rare-variant class (observed type property).

ƒ Individual Ancestry Label Calculated 1 hop from ground truth string DR-59
In RuleSpeak

A genomic variant's individual ancestry label is the ancestry label of the genomic variant's individual.

In English

The ancestry label of the linked individual.

As a formula
= INDEX(Ancestry Label, MATCH(Individual, Individual ID, 0))
Inputs 3 fields this rule reads

Ground truth at the leaves 3 the raw values this ultimately depends on
● Ancestry Label Individuals ● Individual GenomicVariants ● Individual ID Individuals

Lookup of individual ancestry for stratification checks.

ƒ Is Rare Variant Calculated 1 hop from ground truth boolean DR-60
In RuleSpeak

The genomic variant's is rare variant is determined by the following priority:

  1. true if the allele frequency is less than 0.01
  2. false otherwise
In English

If allele frequency is less than 0.01 then true(), otherwise false().

As a formula
= IF(Allele Frequency < 0.01, TRUE(), FALSE())
Inputs 1 fields this rule reads
●Allele FrequencyGenomicVariants Population allele frequency for rare-variant burden.
Consumers 1 direct +34 transitive rules that read this one
ƒ Is Causal Candidate GenomicVariants Variant is Causal Candidate CausalMechanisms Is Causal Architecture Node CausalMechanisms Count Confirmed Causal Nodes Individuals Sum Confirmed Causal Confidence Individuals Is Mechanism Matched Treatments Is Ancestry Transportable CausalMechanisms Individual Confirmed Node Count IndividualPredictions Individual Causal Mass IndividualPredictions Is Treatment Response Predicted Treatments Treatment Response Deciding Factor Treatments Count Cross Ancestry Confirmed Nodes Individuals Predicted Value IndividualPredictions Rests on Confirmed Mechanism IndividualPredictions Is Falsifiability Backed IndividualPredictions Count Predicted Treatment Responses Individuals Individual Cross Ancestry Node Count IndividualPredictions Patient Stratification Tier IndividualPredictions Is Clinically Actionable IndividualPredictions Lifecycle State Key IndividualPredictions Has Spurious Correlation Flag IndividualPredictions Is Severity Actionable IndividualPredictions Severity Deciding Factor IndividualPredictions Treatment Response Deciding Factor IndividualPredictions Deciding Gate IndividualPredictions Recommended Treatment Line IndividualPredictions Treatment Line Deciding Factor IndividualPredictions Has Predicted Treatment Response Individuals Is Transportable to Absent Ancestry IndividualPredictions Progression Vs Actionability Disagree IndividualPredictions Is High Confidence Prediction IndividualPredictions Individual Has Predicted Treatment Response IndividualPredictions Is Ancestry Transport Safe IndividualPredictions Transport Gate Status IndividualPredictions Is Treatment Response Actionable IndividualPredictions
Ground truth at the leaves 1 the raw values this ultimately depends on
● Allele Frequency GenomicVariants

True when allele frequency below 0.01.

ƒ Is Causal Candidate Calculated 2 hops from ground truth boolean DR-61
In RuleSpeak

The genomic variant's is causal candidate is determined by the following priority:

  1. true if all of these hold:
    • any of these hold:
      • the is rare variant flag is set
      • the variant class is rare flag is set
    • the has allele specific expression flag is set
  2. false otherwise
In English

If is rare variant or variant class is rare and has allele specific expression then true(), otherwise false().

As a formula
= IF(AND(OR(Is Rare Variant, Variant Class is Rare), Has Allele Specific Expression), TRUE(), FALSE())
Inputs 3 fields this rule reads
ƒIs Rare VariantGenomicVariants If allele frequency is less than 0.01 then true(), otherwise false(). ↗Variant Class is RareGenomicVariants The is rare variant class of the linked variant type. ●Has Allele Specific ExpressionGenomicVariants True when allele-specific expression observed.
Consumers 1 direct +33 transitive rules that read this one
↗ Variant is Causal Candidate CausalMechanisms Is Causal Architecture Node CausalMechanisms Count Confirmed Causal Nodes Individuals Sum Confirmed Causal Confidence Individuals Is Mechanism Matched Treatments Is Ancestry Transportable CausalMechanisms Individual Confirmed Node Count IndividualPredictions Individual Causal Mass IndividualPredictions Is Treatment Response Predicted Treatments Treatment Response Deciding Factor Treatments Count Cross Ancestry Confirmed Nodes Individuals Predicted Value IndividualPredictions Rests on Confirmed Mechanism IndividualPredictions Is Falsifiability Backed IndividualPredictions Count Predicted Treatment Responses Individuals Individual Cross Ancestry Node Count IndividualPredictions Patient Stratification Tier IndividualPredictions Is Clinically Actionable IndividualPredictions Lifecycle State Key IndividualPredictions Has Spurious Correlation Flag IndividualPredictions Is Severity Actionable IndividualPredictions Severity Deciding Factor IndividualPredictions Treatment Response Deciding Factor IndividualPredictions Deciding Gate IndividualPredictions Recommended Treatment Line IndividualPredictions Treatment Line Deciding Factor IndividualPredictions Has Predicted Treatment Response Individuals Is Transportable to Absent Ancestry IndividualPredictions Progression Vs Actionability Disagree IndividualPredictions Is High Confidence Prediction IndividualPredictions Individual Has Predicted Treatment Response IndividualPredictions Is Ancestry Transport Safe IndividualPredictions Transport Gate Status IndividualPredictions Is Treatment Response Actionable IndividualPredictions
Ground truth at the leaves 5 the raw values this ultimately depends on
● Allele Frequency GenomicVariants ● Is Rare Variant Class VariantTypes ● Variant Type GenomicVariants ● Variant Type ID VariantTypes ● Has Allele Specific Expression GenomicVariants

Derived: rare (by frequency or class) AND shows allele-specific expression.

● Source Quote Ground truth string

Ground truth. Written directly to the database (datatype string); not computed from anything else. Everything derived ultimately traces back to values like this one.

PANEL 2->1 provenance pointer (raw leaf, read by nothing downstream): the literal span of the patient's CaseNarrative from which this variant's raw value was extracted. A human verifies the EXTRACTION was faithful here, separately from trusting the derived diagnosis — which is exactly what defeats 'a hallucination laundered through a deterministic function'.

Omics Assay

Omics assay instances linking individuals to modalities and tissues; captures batch effects, measurement error, and missing tissues.

● Assay Label Ground truth string

Ground truth. Written directly to the database (datatype string); not computed from anything else. Everything derived ultimately traces back to values like this one.

Human-readable assay label.

● Individual Ground truth string

Ground truth. Written directly to the database (datatype string); not computed from anything else. Everything derived ultimately traces back to values like this one.

Individual assayed.

● Omics Modality Ground truth string

Ground truth. Written directly to the database (datatype string); not computed from anything else. Everything derived ultimately traces back to values like this one.

Omics modality used.

● Tissue Ground truth string

Ground truth. Written directly to the database (datatype string); not computed from anything else. Everything derived ultimately traces back to values like this one.

Tissue sampled; empty when missing tissues.

● Federated Dataset Ground truth string

Ground truth. Written directly to the database (datatype string); not computed from anything else. Everything derived ultimately traces back to values like this one.

Source federated node if applicable.

● Measurement Error Score Ground truth number

Ground truth. Written directly to the database (datatype number); not computed from anything else. Everything derived ultimately traces back to values like this one.

Estimated measurement error (0-1).

● Has Cell State Specific Effect Ground truth boolean

Ground truth. Written directly to the database (datatype boolean); not computed from anything else. Everything derived ultimately traces back to values like this one.

True when cell-state-specific effects detected.

ƒ Name Calculated 1 hop from ground truth string DR-62
In RuleSpeak

An omics assay's name is computed as the assay label.

In English

Assay label.

As a formula
= Assay Label

Ground truth at the leaves 1 the raw values this ultimately depends on

Display label.

An omics assay's parent path is the relative path of the omics assay's individual.

The relative path of the linked individual.

= INDEX(Relative Path, MATCH(Individual, Individual ID, 0))
Inputs 3 fields this rule reads

Ground truth at the leaves 4 the raw values this ultimately depends on
● Family Name Individuals ● Given Name Individuals ● Individual OmicsAssays ● Individual ID Individuals

Lookup: Individuals.RelativePath via Individual — used to chain this entity's path under its parent.

ƒ Relative Path Calculated 4 hops from ground truth string DR-64
In RuleSpeak

An omics assay's relative path is computed as the parent path, followed by the literal “/assays/”, followed by the omics assay ID.

In English

(parent path joined with "/assays/") joined with omics assay id.

As a formula
= Parent Path & "/assays/" & Omics Assay ID
Inputs 2 fields this rule reads
↗Parent PathOmicsAssays The relative path of the linked individual. ●Omics Assay IDOmicsAssays Stable identifier for each omics assay.

● Family Name Individuals ● Given Name Individuals ● Individual OmicsAssays ● Individual ID Individuals ● Omics Assay ID OmicsAssays

Path to this OmicsAssay page, chained under its Individual parent.

ƒ Modality Label Calculated 1 hop from ground truth string DR-65
In RuleSpeak

An omics assay's modality label is the modality label of the omics assay's omics modality.

In English

The modality label of the linked omics modality.

As a formula
= INDEX(Modality Label, MATCH(Omics Modality, Omics Modality ID, 0))
Inputs 3 fields this rule reads
●Modality LabelOmicsModalities Human-readable modality name. ●Omics ModalityOmicsAssays Omics modality used. ●Omics Modality IDOmicsModalities Stable identifier for each omics modality.
Ground truth at the leaves 3 the raw values this ultimately depends on
● Modality Label OmicsModalities ● Omics Modality OmicsAssays ● Omics Modality ID OmicsModalities

Lookup of modality label.

ƒ Tissue Label Calculated 1 hop from ground truth string DR-66
In RuleSpeak

The omics assay's tissue label is determined by the following priority:

  1. the literal “Missing Tissue” if the tissue is blank
  2. the tissue label of the omics assay's tissue otherwise
In English

If tissue is "" then "Missing Tissue", otherwise the tissue label of the linked tissue.

As a formula
= IF(Tissue = "", "Missing Tissue", INDEX(Tissue Label, MATCH(Tissue, Tissue ID, 0)))
Inputs 3 fields this rule reads
●TissueOmicsAssays Tissue sampled; empty when missing tissues. ●Tissue LabelTissues Human-readable tissue name (blood, gut, synovium, skin, brain). ●Tissue IDTissues Stable identifier for each tissue type.
Ground truth at the leaves 3 the raw values this ultimately depends on
● Tissue OmicsAssays ● Tissue Label Tissues ● Tissue ID Tissues

Lookup of tissue label.

ƒ Has Batch Effect Risk Calculated 1 hop from ground truth boolean DR-67
In RuleSpeak

The omics assay's has batch effect risk is determined by the following priority:

  1. true if the measurement error score is greater than 0.3
  2. false otherwise
In English

If measurement error score is greater than 0.3 then true(), otherwise false().

As a formula
= IF(Measurement Error Score > 0.3, TRUE(), FALSE())
Inputs 1 fields this rule reads
●Measurement Error ScoreOmicsAssays Estimated measurement error (0-1).
Consumers 1 direct +40 transitive rules that read this one
ƒ Is High Quality Assay OmicsAssays Assay is High Quality EvidenceItems Is Qualified Evidence EvidenceItems Count Qualified Evidence CausalMechanisms Count Modalities Supporting CausalMechanisms Is Experimentally Falsifiable CausalMechanisms Causal Confidence CausalMechanisms Is Spurious Derived CausalMechanisms Is Causal Architecture Node CausalMechanisms Sum Confirmed Causal Confidence Individuals Count Confirmed Causal Nodes Individuals Is Mechanism Matched Treatments Is Ancestry Transportable CausalMechanisms Individual Causal Mass IndividualPredictions Individual Confirmed Node Count IndividualPredictions Is Treatment Response Predicted Treatments Treatment Response Deciding Factor Treatments Count Cross Ancestry Confirmed Nodes Individuals Predicted Value IndividualPredictions Rests on Confirmed Mechanism IndividualPredictions Is Falsifiability Backed IndividualPredictions Count Predicted Treatment Responses Individuals Individual Cross Ancestry Node Count IndividualPredictions Patient Stratification Tier IndividualPredictions Is Clinically Actionable IndividualPredictions Lifecycle State Key IndividualPredictions Has Spurious Correlation Flag IndividualPredictions Is Severity Actionable IndividualPredictions Severity Deciding Factor IndividualPredictions Treatment Response Deciding Factor IndividualPredictions Deciding Gate IndividualPredictions Recommended Treatment Line IndividualPredictions Treatment Line Deciding Factor IndividualPredictions Has Predicted Treatment Response Individuals Is Transportable to Absent Ancestry IndividualPredictions Progression Vs Actionability Disagree IndividualPredictions Is High Confidence Prediction IndividualPredictions Individual Has Predicted Treatment Response IndividualPredictions Is Ancestry Transport Safe IndividualPredictions Transport Gate Status IndividualPredictions Is Treatment Response Actionable IndividualPredictions
Ground truth at the leaves 1 the raw values this ultimately depends on
● Measurement Error Score OmicsAssays

True when measurement error exceeds 0.3.

ƒ Is High Quality Assay Calculated 2 hops from ground truth boolean DR-68
In RuleSpeak

The omics assay's is high quality assay is determined by the following priority:

  1. true if all of these hold:
    • it is not the case that the has batch effect risk flag is set
    • the measurement error score is less than 0.15
  2. false otherwise
In English

If not has batch effect risk and measurement error score is less than 0.15 then true(), otherwise false().

As a formula
= IF(AND(NOT(Has Batch Effect Risk), Measurement Error Score < 0.15), TRUE(), FALSE())
Inputs 2 fields this rule reads
ƒHas Batch Effect RiskOmicsAssays If measurement error score is greater than 0.3 then true(), otherwise false(). ●Measurement Error ScoreOmicsAssays Estimated measurement error (0-1).
Consumers 1 direct +39 transitive rules that read this one

● Measurement Error Score OmicsAssays

2nd-order quality flag.

⇢ Evidence Items Relationship string

A pointer to Evidence Item. It is not derived from anything else — it is the link the rest of the DAG hangs off.

Evidence items measured in this assay.

Evidence Item

One observed support signal for a causal mechanism, measured by one omics assay. Mechanism confidence is an aggregation over these rows.

● Evidence Label Ground truth string

Ground truth. Written directly to the database (datatype string); not computed from anything else. Everything derived ultimately traces back to values like this one.

Human label for the evidence row.

● Causal Mechanism Ground truth string

Ground truth. Written directly to the database (datatype string); not computed from anything else. Everything derived ultimately traces back to values like this one.

FK to the mechanism this evidence supports.

● Omics Assay Ground truth string

Ground truth. Written directly to the database (datatype string); not computed from anything else. Everything derived ultimately traces back to values like this one.

FK to the omics assay this signal was measured in.

● Effect Size Ground truth number

Ground truth. Written directly to the database (datatype number); not computed from anything else. Everything derived ultimately traces back to values like this one.

OBSERVATION: measured (absolute) effect magnitude.

● Standard Error Ground truth number

Ground truth. Written directly to the database (datatype number); not computed from anything else. Everything derived ultimately traces back to values like this one.

OBSERVATION: measured standard error of the effect.

● Is Cross Modality Ground truth boolean

Ground truth. Written directly to the database (datatype boolean); not computed from anything else. Everything derived ultimately traces back to values like this one.

OBSERVATION: TRUE if this signal comes from a different omics modality than the mechanism's primary one.

● Is Negative Control Arm Ground truth boolean

Ground truth. Written directly to the database (datatype boolean); not computed from anything else. Everything derived ultimately traces back to values like this one.

OBSERVATION: TRUE if this row is a measured control arm rather than support.

● Is Adjusted for Ancestry P Cs Ground truth boolean

Ground truth. Written directly to the database (datatype boolean); not computed from anything else. Everything derived ultimately traces back to values like this one.

OBSERVATION: TRUE if the analysis adjusted for ancestry principal components.

● Is Adjusted for Batch Ground truth boolean

Ground truth. Written directly to the database (datatype boolean); not computed from anything else. Everything derived ultimately traces back to values like this one.

OBSERVATION: TRUE if the analysis adjusted for batch.

● Is Synthetic Leaf Ground truth boolean

Ground truth. Written directly to the database (datatype boolean); not computed from anything else. Everything derived ultimately traces back to values like this one.

TRUST BOUNDARY (raw leaf, read by nothing downstream): TRUE marks this row as an LLM-produced synthetic-but-transparent test result rather than a real measured observation. Surfaces in the writeup so legible-reasoning-over-synthetic-evidence is never misread as solved science. The case is invented; this flag says so out loud at the cell level.

● Represents Assay Modality Ground truth string

Ground truth. Written directly to the database (datatype string); not computed from anything else. Everything derived ultimately traces back to values like this one.

PROVENANCE STUB (raw leaf, read by nothing downstream): which real assay/modality this synthetic leaf stands in for (e.g. 'cis-eQTL, whole-blood RNA-seq'). Documents where real evidence would attach if this case were instrumented.

● Identification Assumption Ground truth string

Ground truth. Written directly to the database (datatype string); not computed from anything else. Everything derived ultimately traces back to values like this one.

PROVENANCE STUB (raw leaf, read by nothing downstream): the identification assumption under which this leaf would be valid evidence (e.g. 'no horizontal pleiotropy; instrument relevance F>10'). Turns 'synthetic test result' into 'documentation of where real evidence attaches and under what condition it is trustworthy'.

ƒ Name Calculated 1 hop from ground truth string DR-69
In RuleSpeak

An evidence item's name is computed as the evidence label.

In English

Evidence label.

As a formula
= Evidence Label
Inputs 1 fields this rule reads
●Evidence LabelEvidenceItems Human label for the evidence row.
Ground truth at the leaves 1 the raw values this ultimately depends on
● Evidence Label EvidenceItems

Display name.

↗ Parent Path Lookup 5 hops from ground truth string DR-70
In RuleSpeak

An evidence item's parent path is the relative path of the evidence item's causal mechanism.

The relative path of the linked causal mechanism.

As a formula
= INDEX(Relative Path, MATCH(Causal Mechanism, Causal Mechanism ID, 0))
Inputs 3 fields this rule reads

Consumers 1 direct rules that read this one
ƒ Relative Path EvidenceItems
Ground truth at the leaves 6 the raw values this ultimately depends on

Lookup: CausalMechanisms.RelativePath via CausalMechanism — used to chain this entity's path under its parent.

ƒ Relative Path Calculated 6 hops from ground truth string DR-71
In RuleSpeak

An evidence item's relative path is computed as the parent path, followed by the literal “/evidence/”, followed by the evidence item ID.

In English

(parent path joined with "/evidence/") joined with evidence item id.

As a formula
= Parent Path & "/evidence/" & Evidence Item ID
Inputs 2 fields this rule reads
↗Parent PathEvidenceItems The relative path of the linked causal mechanism. ●Evidence Item IDEvidenceItems Stable id for this evidence observation.
Ground truth at the leaves 7 the raw values this ultimately depends on

Path to this EvidenceItem page, chained under its CausalMechanism parent.

↗ Assay is High Quality Lookup 3 hops from ground truth boolean DR-72
In RuleSpeak

An evidence item's assay is high quality is true when the evidence item's omics assay is a high quality assay.

In English

The is high quality assay of the linked omics assay.

As a formula
= INDEX(Is High Quality Assay, MATCH(Omics Assay, Omics Assay ID, 0))
Inputs 3 fields this rule reads
ƒIs High Quality AssayOmicsAssays If not has batch effect risk and measurement error score is less than 0.15 then true(), otherwise false(). ●Omics AssayEvidenceItems FK to the omics assay this signal was measured in. ●Omics Assay IDOmicsAssays Stable identifier for each omics assay.

● Measurement Error Score OmicsAssays ● Omics Assay EvidenceItems ● Omics Assay ID OmicsAssays

Whether the supporting assay passed quality control.

ƒ Z Stat Calculated 1 hop from ground truth number DR-73
In RuleSpeak

The evidence item's z stat is determined by the following priority:

  1. the effect size divided by the standard error if the standard error is greater than 0
  2. 0 otherwise
In English

If standard error is greater than 0 then (effect size divided by standard error), otherwise 0.

As a formula
= IF(Standard Error > 0, Effect Size / Standard Error, 0)
Inputs 2 fields this rule reads
●Standard ErrorEvidenceItems OBSERVATION: measured standard error of the effect. ●Effect SizeEvidenceItems OBSERVATION: measured (absolute) effect magnitude.
Consumers 1 direct +38 transitive rules that read this one

Derived signal-to-noise ratio (effect / SE), 0 if SE non-positive.

ƒ Is Confound Controlled Calculated 1 hop from ground truth boolean DR-74
In RuleSpeak

The evidence item's is confound controlled is determined by the following priority:

  1. true if all of these hold:
    • the is adjusted for ancestry p cs flag is set
    • the is adjusted for batch flag is set
  2. false otherwise
In English

If is adjusted for ancestry p cs and is adjusted for batch then true(), otherwise false().

As a formula
= IF(AND(Is Adjusted for Ancestry P Cs, Is Adjusted for Batch), TRUE(), FALSE())
Inputs 2 fields this rule reads
●Is Adjusted for Ancestry P CsEvidenceItems OBSERVATION: TRUE if the analysis adjusted for ancestry principal components. ●Is Adjusted for BatchEvidenceItems OBSERVATION: TRUE if the analysis adjusted for batch.

Derived: both ancestry-PC and batch adjustment present.

ƒ Is Qualified Evidence Calculated 4 hops from ground truth boolean DR-75
In RuleSpeak

The evidence item's is qualified evidence is determined by the following priority:

  1. true if all of these hold:
    • the assay is high quality flag is set
    • it is not the case that the is negative control arm flag is set
    • the z stat is at least 2
    • the is confound controlled flag is set
  2. false otherwise
In English

If assay is high quality, not is negative control arm, z stat is at least 2, and is confound controlled then true(), otherwise false().

As a formula
= IF(AND(Assay is High Quality, NOT(Is Negative Control Arm), Z Stat >= 2, Is Confound Controlled), TRUE(), FALSE())
Inputs 4 fields this rule reads
↗Assay is High QualityEvidenceItems The is high quality assay of the linked omics assay. ●Is Negative Control ArmEvidenceItems OBSERVATION: TRUE if this row is a measured control arm rather than support. ƒZ StatEvidenceItems If standard error is greater than 0 then (effect size divided by standard error), otherwise 0. ƒIs Confound ControlledEvidenceItems If is adjusted for ancestry p cs and is adjusted for batch then true(), otherwise false().
Consumers 2 direct +36 transitive rules that read this one

Derived: clean assay, real support arm, signal-to-noise >= 2, confound-controlled.

● Source Quote Ground truth string

Ground truth. Written directly to the database (datatype string); not computed from anything else. Everything derived ultimately traces back to values like this one.

PANEL 2->1 provenance pointer (raw leaf, read by nothing downstream): the literal span of the patient's CaseNarrative from which this evidence assay's raw value was extracted. A human verifies the EXTRACTION was faithful here, separately from trusting the derived diagnosis — which is exactly what defeats 'a hallucination laundered through a deterministic function'.

Cohort Replication

One re-test of a causal mechanism in another federated cohort. Replication and cross-ancestry transport are aggregations over these rows.

● Replication Label Ground truth string

Ground truth. Written directly to the database (datatype string); not computed from anything else. Everything derived ultimately traces back to values like this one.

Human label.

● Causal Mechanism Ground truth string

Ground truth. Written directly to the database (datatype string); not computed from anything else. Everything derived ultimately traces back to values like this one.

FK to the mechanism being re-tested.

● Federated Dataset Ground truth string

Ground truth. Written directly to the database (datatype string); not computed from anything else. Everything derived ultimately traces back to values like this one.

FK to the cohort node the re-test ran in.

● Replication Effect Sign Ground truth integer

Ground truth. Written directly to the database (datatype integer); not computed from anything else. Everything derived ultimately traces back to values like this one.

OBSERVATION: sign of the re-estimated effect (+1 / -1 / 0).

● Replication P Value Ground truth number

Ground truth. Written directly to the database (datatype number); not computed from anything else. Everything derived ultimately traces back to values like this one.

OBSERVATION: p-value of the re-test.

● Replication Ancestry Label Ground truth string

Ground truth. Written directly to the database (datatype string); not computed from anything else. Everything derived ultimately traces back to values like this one.

OBSERVATION: ancestry of the cohort the re-test ran in.

ƒ Replicated At Nominal Sig Calculated 1 hop from ground truth boolean DR-79
In RuleSpeak

The cohort replication's replicated at nominal sig is determined by the following priority:

  1. true if all of these hold:
    • the replication p value is at most 0.05
    • the replication effect sign is 1
  2. false otherwise
In English

If replication p value is at most 0.05 and replication effect sign equals 1 then true(), otherwise false().

As a formula
= IF(AND(Replication P Value <= 0.05, Replication Effect Sign = 1), TRUE(), FALSE())
Inputs 2 fields this rule reads
●Replication P ValueCohortReplications OBSERVATION: p-value of the re-test. ●Replication Effect SignCohortReplications OBSERVATION: sign of the re-estimated effect (+1 / -1 / 0).

Negative Control Test

One stratification / permutation control on a causal mechanism. A true causal signal collapses under the control.

● Control Label Ground truth string

Ground truth. Written directly to the database (datatype string); not computed from anything else. Everything derived ultimately traces back to values like this one.

Human label.

● Causal Mechanism Ground truth string

Ground truth. Written directly to the database (datatype string); not computed from anything else. Everything derived ultimately traces back to values like this one.

FK to the mechanism being controlled.

● Test Kind Ground truth string

Ground truth. Written directly to the database (datatype string); not computed from anything else. Everything derived ultimately traces back to values like this one.

OBSERVATION: kind of control (ancestry-permutation / batch-stratified / negative-control).

● Permutation Effect Size Ground truth number

Ground truth. Written directly to the database (datatype number); not computed from anything else. Everything derived ultimately traces back to values like this one.

OBSERVATION: (absolute) effect measured under the null/permuted control.

● Null Threshold Ground truth number

Ground truth. Written directly to the database (datatype number); not computed from anything else. Everything derived ultimately traces back to values like this one.

OBSERVATION: pre-registered null-band half-width the control must stay within.

The negative control test's is survived is determined by the following priority:

  2. false otherwise
