● Source Quote Ground truth string

Ground truth. Written directly to the database (datatype string); not computed from anything else. Everything derived ultimately traces back to values like this one.

PANEL 2->1 provenance pointer (raw leaf, read by nothing downstream): the literal span of the patient's CaseNarrative from which this negative control's raw value was extracted. A human verifies the EXTRACTION was faithful here, separately from trusting the derived diagnosis — which is exactly what defeats 'a hallucination laundered through a deterministic function'.

Environmental Exposure

Longitudinal environmental exposures contributing to gene-environment-microbiome interactions and shifting environments.

● Exposure Label Ground truth string

Ground truth. Written directly to the database (datatype string); not computed from anything else. Everything derived ultimately traces back to values like this one.

Exposure name (smoking, UV, particulate, etc.).

● Individual Ground truth string

Ground truth. Written directly to the database (datatype string); not computed from anything else. Everything derived ultimately traces back to values like this one.

Exposed individual.

● Exposure Level Ground truth number

Ground truth. Written directly to the database (datatype number); not computed from anything else. Everything derived ultimately traces back to values like this one.

Quantified exposure intensity.

● Exposure Start Date Ground truth datetime

Ground truth. Written directly to the database (datatype datetime); not computed from anything else. Everything derived ultimately traces back to values like this one.

Start of longitudinal exposure window.

● Is Maternal Effect Ground truth boolean

Ground truth. Written directly to the database (datatype boolean); not computed from anything else. Everything derived ultimately traces back to values like this one.

True when exposure reflects maternal developmental effects.

ƒ Is High Exposure Calculated 1 hop from ground truth boolean DR-91
In RuleSpeak

The environmental exposure's is high exposure is determined by the following priority:

  1. true if the exposure level is greater than 5
  2. false otherwise

If exposure level is greater than 5 then true(), otherwise false().

As a formula
= IF(Exposure Level > 5, TRUE(), FALSE())

True when exposure level exceeds threshold.

Treatment

Treatment histories capturing treatment-induced changes, treatment response, and adverse effects.

● Treatment Label Ground truth string

Ground truth. Written directly to the database (datatype string); not computed from anything else. Everything derived ultimately traces back to values like this one.

Therapy name.

● Individual Ground truth string

Ground truth. Written directly to the database (datatype string); not computed from anything else. Everything derived ultimately traces back to values like this one.

Treated individual.

● Autoimmune Disease Ground truth string

Ground truth. Written directly to the database (datatype string); not computed from anything else. Everything derived ultimately traces back to values like this one.

Target autoimmune disease.

● Targets Mechanism Ground truth string

Ground truth. Written directly to the database (datatype string); not computed from anything else. Everything derived ultimately traces back to values like this one.

FK to the CausalMechanism this treatment acts on (the drug's target mechanism). Raw leaf: which mechanism the clinician/LLM says this therapy targets.

● Treatment Response Ground truth string

Ground truth. Written directly to the database (datatype string); not computed from anything else. Everything derived ultimately traces back to values like this one.

Response category: Complete, Partial, None, Adverse.

● Has Treatment Induced Change Ground truth boolean

Ground truth. Written directly to the database (datatype boolean); not computed from anything else. Everything derived ultimately traces back to values like this one.

True when molecular state shifted due to therapy.

● Has Adverse Effect Ground truth boolean

Ground truth. Written directly to the database (datatype boolean); not computed from anything else. Everything derived ultimately traces back to values like this one.

True when adverse effects observed.

● Start Date Ground truth datetime

Ground truth. Written directly to the database (datatype datetime); not computed from anything else. Everything derived ultimately traces back to values like this one.

Treatment start date.

Treatment label.

(parent path joined with "/treatments/") joined with treatment id.

In RuleSpeak

A treatment's autoimmune disease label is the disease label of the treatment's autoimmune disease.

In English

The disease label of the linked autoimmune disease.

As a formula
= INDEX(Disease Label, MATCH(Autoimmune Disease, Autoimmune Disease ID, 0))
Inputs 3 fields this rule reads
●Disease LabelAutoimmuneDiseases Human-readable disease name. ●Autoimmune DiseaseTreatments Target autoimmune disease. ●Autoimmune Disease IDAutoimmuneDiseases Stable identifier for each autoimmune disease (e.g. sle, ra, t1d).
Ground truth at the leaves 3 the raw values this ultimately depends on
● Disease Label AutoimmuneDiseases ● Autoimmune Disease Treatments ● Autoimmune Disease ID AutoimmuneDiseases

Disease label lookup.

ƒ Is Effective Treatment Calculated 1 hop from ground truth boolean DR-96
In RuleSpeak

The treatment's is effective treatment is determined by the following priority:

  1. true if all of these hold:
    • any of these hold:
      • the treatment response is the literal “Complete”
      • the treatment response is the literal “Partial”
    • it is not the case that the has adverse effect flag is set
  2. false otherwise
In English

If treatment response is "Complete" or treatment response is "Partial" and not has adverse effect then true(), otherwise false().

As a formula
= IF(AND(OR(Treatment Response = "Complete", Treatment Response = "Partial"), NOT(Has Adverse Effect)), TRUE(), FALSE())
Inputs 2 fields this rule reads
●Treatment ResponseTreatments Response category: Complete, Partial, None, Adverse. ●Has Adverse EffectTreatments True when adverse effects observed.
Consumers 1 direct +6 transitive rules that read this one
ƒ Is Treatment Response Predicted Treatments Count Predicted Treatment Responses Individuals Treatment Response Deciding Factor Treatments Has Predicted Treatment Response Individuals Individual Has Predicted Treatment Response IndividualPredictions Is Treatment Response Actionable IndividualPredictions Treatment Response Deciding Factor IndividualPredictions
Ground truth at the leaves 2 the raw values this ultimately depends on
● Treatment Response Treatments ● Has Adverse Effect Treatments

True for Complete or Partial response without adverse effects.

↗ Is Mechanism Matched Lookup 8 hops from ground truth boolean DR-97
In RuleSpeak

The treatment's is mechanism matched is determined by the following priority:

  1. false if the targets mechanism is blank
  2. the is causal architecture node of the treatment's targets mechanism otherwise
In English

If targets mechanism is "" then false(), otherwise the is causal architecture node of the linked targets mechanism.

As a formula
= IF(Targets Mechanism = "", FALSE(), INDEX(Is Causal Architecture Node, MATCH(Targets Mechanism, Causal Mechanism ID, 0)))
Inputs 3 fields this rule reads
●Targets MechanismTreatments FK to the CausalMechanism this treatment acts on (the drug's target mechanism). Raw leaf: which mechanism the clinician/LLM says this therapy targets. ƒIs Causal Architecture NodeCausalMechanisms If causal confidence is at least 0.7, is experimentally falsifiable, not is spurious derived, and variant is causal candidate or environmental exposure is not "" then true(), otherwise false(). ●Causal Mechanism IDCausalMechanisms Stable identifier for each causal mechanism hypothesis.
Consumers 2 direct +5 transitive rules that read this one
ƒ Is Treatment Response Predicted Treatments ƒ Treatment Response Deciding Factor Treatments Count Predicted Treatment Responses Individuals Has Predicted Treatment Response Individuals Individual Has Predicted Treatment Response IndividualPredictions Is Treatment Response Actionable IndividualPredictions Treatment Response Deciding Factor IndividualPredictions
Ground truth at the leaves 28 the raw values this ultimately depends on
● Targets Mechanism Treatments ● Causal Mechanism EvidenceItems ● Causal Mechanism ID CausalMechanisms ● Measurement Error Score OmicsAssays ● Omics Assay EvidenceItems ● Omics Assay ID OmicsAssays ● Is Negative Control Arm EvidenceItems ● Standard Error EvidenceItems ● Effect Size EvidenceItems ● Is Adjusted for Ancestry P Cs EvidenceItems ● Is Adjusted for Batch EvidenceItems ● Is Cross Modality EvidenceItems ● Causal Mechanism CohortReplications ● Replication P Value CohortReplications ● Replication Effect Sign CohortReplications ● Causal Mechanism NegativeControlTests ● Permutation Effect Size NegativeControlTests ● Null Threshold NegativeControlTests ● Causal Mechanism InterventionTargets ● Has Pleiotropy CausalMechanisms ● Genomic Variant CausalMechanisms ● Allele Frequency GenomicVariants ● Is Rare Variant Class VariantTypes ● Variant Type GenomicVariants ● Variant Type ID VariantTypes ● Has Allele Specific Expression GenomicVariants ● Genomic Variant ID GenomicVariants ● Environmental Exposure CausalMechanisms

True when the treatment's target mechanism is a CONFIRMED causal-architecture node (empty-guarded). This is the 'mechanism match'.

ƒ Is Treatment Response Predicted Calculated 9 hops from ground truth boolean DR-98
In RuleSpeak

The treatment's is treatment response predicted is determined by the following priority:

  1. true if all of these hold:
    • the is effective treatment flag is set
    • the is mechanism matched flag is set
  2. false otherwise
In English

If is effective treatment and is mechanism matched then true(), otherwise false().

As a formula
= IF(AND(Is Effective Treatment, Is Mechanism Matched), TRUE(), FALSE())
Inputs 2 fields this rule reads
ƒIs Effective TreatmentTreatments If treatment response is "Complete" or treatment response is "Partial" and not has adverse effect then true(), otherwise false(). ↗Is Mechanism MatchedTreatments If targets mechanism is "" then false(), otherwise the is causal architecture node of the linked targets mechanism.
Consumers 2 direct +4 transitive rules that read this one
Σ Count Predicted Treatment Responses Individuals ƒ Treatment Response Deciding Factor Treatments Has Predicted Treatment Response Individuals Individual Has Predicted Treatment Response IndividualPredictions Is Treatment Response Actionable IndividualPredictions Treatment Response Deciding Factor IndividualPredictions
Ground truth at the leaves 30 the raw values this ultimately depends on
● Treatment Response Treatments ● Has Adverse Effect Treatments ● Targets Mechanism Treatments ● Causal Mechanism EvidenceItems ● Causal Mechanism ID CausalMechanisms ● Measurement Error Score OmicsAssays ● Omics Assay EvidenceItems ● Omics Assay ID OmicsAssays ● Is Negative Control Arm EvidenceItems ● Standard Error EvidenceItems ● Effect Size EvidenceItems ● Is Adjusted for Ancestry P Cs EvidenceItems ● Is Adjusted for Batch EvidenceItems ● Is Cross Modality EvidenceItems ● Causal Mechanism CohortReplications ● Replication P Value CohortReplications ● Replication Effect Sign CohortReplications ● Causal Mechanism NegativeControlTests ● Permutation Effect Size NegativeControlTests ● Null Threshold NegativeControlTests ● Causal Mechanism InterventionTargets ● Has Pleiotropy CausalMechanisms ● Genomic Variant CausalMechanisms ● Allele Frequency GenomicVariants ● Is Rare Variant Class VariantTypes ● Variant Type GenomicVariants ● Variant Type ID VariantTypes ● Has Allele Specific Expression GenomicVariants ● Genomic Variant ID GenomicVariants ● Environmental Exposure CausalMechanisms

Derived: the treatment is effective AND targets a confirmed mechanism. A drug aimed at a debunked mechanism, or one that didn't respond / was adverse, is NOT predicted.

ƒ Treatment Response Deciding Factor Calculated 10 hops from ground truth string DR-99
In RuleSpeak

The treatment's treatment response deciding factor is determined by the following priority:

  1. the literal “EffectiveOnConfirmedMechanism” if the is treatment response predicted flag is set
  2. the literal “NoConfirmedMechanism” if it is not the case that the is mechanism matched flag is set
  3. the literal “AdverseEffect” if the has adverse effect flag is set
  4. the literal “NoResponse” if any of these hold:
    • the treatment response is the literal “None”
    • the treatment response is the literal “Adverse”
  5. the literal “Undetermined” otherwise
In English

If is treatment response predicted then "EffectiveOnConfirmedMechanism", otherwise if not is mechanism matched then "NoConfirmedMechanism", otherwise if has adverse effect then "AdverseEffect", otherwise if treatment response is "None" or treatment response is "Adverse" then "NoResponse", otherwise "Undetermined".

As a formula
= IF(Is Treatment Response Predicted, "EffectiveOnConfirmedMechanism", IF(NOT(Is Mechanism Matched), "NoConfirmedMechanism", IF(Has Adverse Effect, "AdverseEffect", IF(OR(Treatment Response = "None", Treatment Response = "Adverse"), "NoResponse", "Undetermined"))))
Inputs 4 fields this rule reads
ƒIs Treatment Response PredictedTreatments If is effective treatment and is mechanism matched then true(), otherwise false(). ↗Is Mechanism MatchedTreatments If targets mechanism is "" then false(), otherwise the is causal architecture node of the linked targets mechanism. ●Has Adverse EffectTreatments True when adverse effects observed. ●Treatment ResponseTreatments Response category: Complete, Partial, None, Adverse.
Ground truth at the leaves 30 the raw values this ultimately depends on
● Treatment Response Treatments ● Has Adverse Effect Treatments ● Targets Mechanism Treatments ● Causal Mechanism EvidenceItems ● Causal Mechanism ID CausalMechanisms ● Measurement Error Score OmicsAssays ● Omics Assay EvidenceItems ● Omics Assay ID OmicsAssays ● Is Negative Control Arm EvidenceItems ● Standard Error EvidenceItems ● Effect Size EvidenceItems ● Is Adjusted for Ancestry P Cs EvidenceItems ● Is Adjusted for Batch EvidenceItems ● Is Cross Modality EvidenceItems ● Causal Mechanism CohortReplications ● Replication P Value CohortReplications ● Replication Effect Sign CohortReplications ● Causal Mechanism NegativeControlTests ● Permutation Effect Size NegativeControlTests ● Null Threshold NegativeControlTests ● Causal Mechanism InterventionTargets ● Has Pleiotropy CausalMechanisms ● Genomic Variant CausalMechanisms ● Allele Frequency GenomicVariants ● Is Rare Variant Class VariantTypes ● Variant Type GenomicVariants ● Variant Type ID VariantTypes ● Has Allele Specific Expression GenomicVariants ● Genomic Variant ID GenomicVariants ● Environmental Exposure CausalMechanisms

Why response is/ isn't predicted — the single deciding reason.

Clinical Phenotype

● Phenotype Label Ground truth string

Ground truth. Written directly to the database (datatype string); not computed from anything else. Everything derived ultimately traces back to values like this one.

Phenotype name.

● Individual Ground truth string

Ground truth. Written directly to the database (datatype string); not computed from anything else. Everything derived ultimately traces back to values like this one.

Individual measured.

● Autoimmune Disease Ground truth string

Ground truth. Written directly to the database (datatype string); not computed from anything else. Everything derived ultimately traces back to values like this one.

Related autoimmune disease.

● Disease Stage Ground truth string

Ground truth. Written directly to the database (datatype string); not computed from anything else. Everything derived ultimately traces back to values like this one.

Disease stage at measurement.

● Tissue Ground truth string

Ground truth. Written directly to the database (datatype string); not computed from anything else. Everything derived ultimately traces back to values like this one.

Tissue context if applicable.

● Severity Score Ground truth number

Ground truth. Written directly to the database (datatype number); not computed from anything else. Everything derived ultimately traces back to values like this one.

Disease severity (0-10).

● Measurement Date Ground truth datetime

Ground truth. Written directly to the database (datatype datetime); not computed from anything else. Everything derived ultimately traces back to values like this one.

Date of phenotype assessment.

● Has Immune Dysfunction Ground truth boolean

Ground truth. Written directly to the database (datatype boolean); not computed from anything else. Everything derived ultimately traces back to values like this one.

True when immune dysfunction markers elevated.

Causal Mechanism

Inferred causal mechanisms linking variants, exposures, and molecular state to clinical phenotypes; must be experimentally falsifiable.

● Mechanism Label Ground truth string

Ground truth. Written directly to the database (datatype string); not computed from anything else. Everything derived ultimately traces back to values like this one.

Human-readable mechanism description.

● Individual Ground truth string

Ground truth. Written directly to the database (datatype string); not computed from anything else. Everything derived ultimately traces back to values like this one.

Individual for whom mechanism is inferred.

● Genomic Variant Ground truth string

Ground truth. Written directly to the database (datatype string); not computed from anything else. Everything derived ultimately traces back to values like this one.

Primary genomic variant if applicable.

● Environmental Exposure Ground truth string

Ground truth. Written directly to the database (datatype string); not computed from anything else. Everything derived ultimately traces back to values like this one.

Environmental exposure if gene-environment interaction.

● Mechanism Type Ground truth string

Ground truth. Written directly to the database (datatype string); not computed from anything else. Everything derived ultimately traces back to values like this one.

Type: regulatory, coding, gene-environment-microbiome, epigenetic-inheritance, enhancer-promoter, feedback.

● Target Pathway Ground truth string

Ground truth. Written directly to the database (datatype string); not computed from anything else. Everything derived ultimately traces back to values like this one.

NEW RAW LEAF: druggable pathway this confirmed mechanism implicates (type-I-IFN / B-cell/autoantibody / T-cell-costim / IL-17/23). Drives treatment-line selection. An observation about the mechanism, not derived.

ƒ Target Pathway Code Calculated 1 hop from ground truth number DR-106
In RuleSpeak

The causal mechanism's target pathway code is determined by the following priority:

  1. 1 if the target pathway is the literal “type-I-IFN”
  2. 2 if the target pathway is the literal “B-cell/autoantibody”
  3. 3 if the target pathway is the literal “T-cell-costim”
  4. 4 if the target pathway is the literal “IL-17/23”
  5. 0 otherwise
In English

If target pathway is "type-I-IFN" then 1, otherwise if target pathway is "B-cell/autoantibody" then 2, otherwise if target pathway is "T-cell-costim" then 3, otherwise if target pathway is "IL-17/23" then 4, otherwise 0.

As a formula
= IF(Target Pathway="type-I-IFN",1,IF(Target Pathway="B-cell/autoantibody",2,IF(Target Pathway="T-cell-costim",3,IF(Target Pathway="IL-17/23",4,0))))
Inputs 1 fields this rule reads
●Target PathwayCausalMechanisms NEW RAW LEAF: druggable pathway this confirmed mechanism implicates (type-I-IFN / B-cell/autoantibody / T-cell-costim / IL-17/23). Drives treatment-line selection. An observation about the mechanism, not derived.
Consumers 1 direct +4 transitive rules that read this one
Σ Target Pathway Code Individuals Target Pathway Individuals Individual Target Pathway IndividualPredictions Recommended Treatment Line IndividualPredictions Treatment Line Deciding Factor IndividualPredictions
Ground truth at the leaves 1 the raw values this ultimately depends on
● Target Pathway CausalMechanisms

Numeric encoding of TargetPathway so the individual can resolve it via a MAXIFS aggregation (transpiler-friendly). 1=type-I-IFN 2=B-cell 3=T-cell 4=IL-17/23.

● Has Pleiotropy Ground truth boolean

Ground truth. Written directly to the database (datatype boolean); not computed from anything else. Everything derived ultimately traces back to values like this one.

True when mechanism shows pleiotropy across phenotypes.

ƒ Name Calculated 1 hop from ground truth string DR-107
In RuleSpeak

In English

Mechanism label.

As a formula
= Mechanism Label
Inputs 1 fields this rule reads

● Mechanism Label CausalMechanisms

Display label.

● Family Name Individuals ● Given Name Individuals ● Individual CausalMechanisms ● Individual ID Individuals ● Causal Mechanism ID CausalMechanisms

Path to this CausalMechanism page, chained under its Individual parent.

ƒ Individual Ancestry Label Calculated 1 hop from ground truth string DR-110
In RuleSpeak

A causal mechanism's individual ancestry label is the ancestry label of the causal mechanism's individual.

In English

The ancestry label of the linked individual.

As a formula
= INDEX(Ancestry Label, MATCH(Individual, Individual ID, 0))
Inputs 3 fields this rule reads
●Ancestry LabelIndividuals Ancestry label for population stratification and ancestry-equitable risk prediction. ●IndividualCausalMechanisms Individual for whom mechanism is inferred. ●Individual IDIndividuals Stable identifier for each individual in the million-person cohort.

● Ancestry Label Individuals ● Individual CausalMechanisms ● Individual ID Individuals

Ancestry lookup.

Σ Count Qualified Evidence Aggregation 5 hops from ground truth integer DR-111
In RuleSpeak

A causal mechanism's count qualified evidence is the number of the causal mechanism's evidence items that are qualified evidences.

In English

Count of EvidenceItems whose causal mechanism is causal mechanism id and is qualified evidence is true().

As a formula
= COUNTIFS(Causal Mechanism, Causal Mechanism ID, Is Qualified Evidence, TRUE())
Inputs 3 fields this rule reads
●Causal MechanismEvidenceItems FK to the mechanism this evidence supports. ●Causal Mechanism IDCausalMechanisms Stable identifier for each causal mechanism hypothesis. ƒIs Qualified EvidenceEvidenceItems If assay is high quality, not is negative control arm, z stat is at least 2, and is confound controlled then true(), otherwise false().

Σ Count Modalities Supporting Aggregation 5 hops from ground truth integer DR-112
In RuleSpeak

A causal mechanism's count modalities supporting is the number of the causal mechanism's evidence items that are cross modalities and are qualified evidences.

In English

Count of EvidenceItems whose causal mechanism is causal mechanism id, is cross modality is true(), and is qualified evidence is true().

As a formula
= COUNTIFS(Causal Mechanism, Causal Mechanism ID, Is Cross Modality, TRUE(), Is Qualified Evidence, TRUE())
Inputs 4 fields this rule reads
●Causal MechanismEvidenceItems FK to the mechanism this evidence supports. ●Causal Mechanism IDCausalMechanisms Stable identifier for each causal mechanism hypothesis. ●Is Cross ModalityEvidenceItems OBSERVATION: TRUE if this signal comes from a different omics modality than the mechanism's primary one. ƒIs Qualified EvidenceEvidenceItems If assay is high quality, not is negative control arm, z stat is at least 2, and is confound controlled then true(), otherwise false().

ƒ Is Experimentally Falsifiable Calculated 6 hops from ground truth boolean DR-114
In RuleSpeak

The causal mechanism's is experimentally falsifiable is determined by the following priority:

  1. true if all of these hold:
    • the count intervention targets is at least 1
    • the count qualified evidence is at least 1
  2. false otherwise
In English

If count intervention targets is at least 1 and count qualified evidence is at least 1 then true(), otherwise false().

As a formula
= IF(AND(Count Intervention Targets >= 1, Count Qualified Evidence >= 1), TRUE(), FALSE())
Inputs 2 fields this rule reads
ΣCount Intervention TargetsCausalMechanisms Count of InterventionTargets whose causal mechanism is causal mechanism id. ΣCount Qualified EvidenceCausalMechanisms Count of EvidenceItems whose causal mechanism is causal mechanism id and is qualified evidence is true().

Σ Count Concordant Replications Aggregation 2 hops from ground truth integer DR-116
In RuleSpeak

A causal mechanism's count concordant replications is the number of the causal mechanism's cohort replications that are replicated at nominal sig.

In English

Count of CohortReplications whose causal mechanism is causal mechanism id and replicated at nominal sig is true().

As a formula
= COUNTIFS(Causal Mechanism, Causal Mechanism ID, Replicated At Nominal Sig, TRUE())
Inputs 3 fields this rule reads
●Causal MechanismCohortReplications FK to the mechanism being re-tested. ●Causal Mechanism IDCausalMechanisms Stable identifier for each causal mechanism hypothesis. ƒReplicated At Nominal SigCohortReplications If replication p value is at most 0.05 and replication effect sign equals 1 then true(), otherwise false().

In RuleSpeak

A causal mechanism's count cross ancestry concordant is the number of the causal mechanism's cohort replications that are cross ancestry concordants.

In English

Count of CohortReplications whose causal mechanism is causal mechanism id and is cross ancestry concordant is true().

As a formula
= COUNTIFS(Causal Mechanism, Causal Mechanism ID, Is Cross Ancestry Concordant, TRUE())
Inputs 3 fields this rule reads
●Causal MechanismCohortReplications FK to the mechanism being re-tested. ●Causal Mechanism IDCausalMechanisms Stable identifier for each causal mechanism hypothesis. ƒIs Cross Ancestry ConcordantCohortReplications If replicated at nominal sig and is different ancestry replication then true(), otherwise false().

● Causal Mechanism CohortReplications ● Causal Mechanism ID CausalMechanisms ● Replication P Value CohortReplications ● Replication Effect Sign CohortReplications ● Replication Ancestry Label CohortReplications ● Ancestry Label Individuals ● Individual CausalMechanisms ● Individual ID Individuals

Concordant re-tests that ran in a DIFFERENT ancestry (the transportability measurement).

ƒ Replication Fraction Calculated 3 hops from ground truth number DR-118
In RuleSpeak

The causal mechanism's replication fraction is determined by the following priority:

  1. the count concordant replications divided by the count replications if the count replications is greater than 0
  2. 0 otherwise
In English

If count replications is greater than 0 then (count concordant replications divided by count replications), otherwise 0.

As a formula
= IF(Count Replications > 0, Count Concordant Replications / Count Replications, 0)
Inputs 2 fields this rule reads
ΣCount ReplicationsCausalMechanisms Count of CohortReplications whose causal mechanism is causal mechanism id. ΣCount Concordant ReplicationsCausalMechanisms Count of CohortReplications whose causal mechanism is causal mechanism id and replicated at nominal sig is true().
Consumers 1 direct +33 transitive rules that read this one

● Causal Mechanism CohortReplications ● Causal Mechanism ID CausalMechanisms ● Replication P Value CohortReplications ● Replication Effect Sign CohortReplications

Derived: fraction of re-tests that were concordant (guarded division).

ƒ Replicates Across Cohorts Calculated 3 hops from ground truth boolean DR-119
In RuleSpeak

The causal mechanism's replicates across cohorts is determined by the following priority:

  1. true if all of these hold:
    • the count replications is at least 2
    • the count concordant replications is at least 2
  2. false otherwise
In English

If count replications is at least 2 and count concordant replications is at least 2 then true(), otherwise false().

As a formula
= IF(AND(Count Replications >= 2, Count Concordant Replications >= 2), TRUE(), FALSE())
Inputs 2 fields this rule reads
ΣCount ReplicationsCausalMechanisms Count of CohortReplications whose causal mechanism is causal mechanism id. ΣCount Concordant ReplicationsCausalMechanisms Count of CohortReplications whose causal mechanism is causal mechanism id and replicated at nominal sig is true().

Σ Count Neg Control Survived Aggregation 2 hops from ground truth integer DR-121
In RuleSpeak

A causal mechanism's count neg control survived is the number of the causal mechanism's negative control tests that are survived.

In English

Count of NegativeControlTests whose causal mechanism is causal mechanism id and is survived is true().

As a formula
= COUNTIFS(Causal Mechanism, Causal Mechanism ID, Is Survived, TRUE())

Negative-control tests the mechanism survived (collapsed under the null).

ƒ Survives Negative Controls Calculated 3 hops from ground truth boolean DR-122
In RuleSpeak

The causal mechanism's survives negative controls is determined by the following priority:

  1. true if all of these hold:
    • the count neg control tests is at least 1
    • the count neg control survived is the count neg control tests
  2. false otherwise
In English

If count neg control tests is at least 1 and count neg control survived equals count neg control tests then true(), otherwise false().

As a formula
= IF(AND(Count Neg Control Tests >= 1, Count Neg Control Survived = Count Neg Control Tests), TRUE(), FALSE())
Inputs 2 fields this rule reads
ΣCount Neg Control TestsCausalMechanisms Count of NegativeControlTests whose causal mechanism is causal mechanism id. ΣCount Neg Control SurvivedCausalMechanisms Count of NegativeControlTests whose causal mechanism is causal mechanism id and is survived is true().
Consumers 2 direct +33 transitive rules that read this one
ƒ Is Spurious Derived CausalMechanisms ƒ Causal Confidence CausalMechanisms Is Causal Architecture Node CausalMechanisms Sum Confirmed Causal Confidence Individuals Count Confirmed Causal Nodes Individuals Is Mechanism Matched Treatments Is Ancestry Transportable CausalMechanisms Individual Causal Mass IndividualPredictions Individual Confirmed Node Count IndividualPredictions Is Treatment Response Predicted Treatments Treatment Response Deciding Factor Treatments Count Cross Ancestry Confirmed Nodes Individuals Predicted Value IndividualPredictions Rests on Confirmed Mechanism IndividualPredictions Is Falsifiability Backed IndividualPredictions Count Predicted Treatment Responses Individuals Individual Cross Ancestry Node Count IndividualPredictions Patient Stratification Tier IndividualPredictions Is Clinically Actionable IndividualPredictions Lifecycle State Key IndividualPredictions Has Spurious Correlation Flag IndividualPredictions Is Severity Actionable IndividualPredictions Severity Deciding Factor IndividualPredictions Treatment Response Deciding Factor IndividualPredictions Deciding Gate IndividualPredictions Recommended Treatment Line IndividualPredictions Treatment Line Deciding Factor IndividualPredictions Has Predicted Treatment Response Individuals Is Transportable to Absent Ancestry IndividualPredictions Progression Vs Actionability Disagree IndividualPredictions Is High Confidence Prediction IndividualPredictions Individual Has Predicted Treatment Response IndividualPredictions Is Ancestry Transport Safe IndividualPredictions Transport Gate Status IndividualPredictions Is Treatment Response Actionable IndividualPredictions
Ground truth at the leaves 4 the raw values this ultimately depends on
● Causal Mechanism NegativeControlTests ● Causal Mechanism ID CausalMechanisms ● Permutation Effect Size NegativeControlTests ● Null Threshold NegativeControlTests

Derived: at least one control run AND all of them survived.

ƒ Is Spurious Derived Calculated 6 hops from ground truth boolean DR-123
In RuleSpeak

The causal mechanism's is spurious derived is determined by the following priority:

  1. true if any of these hold:
    • it is not the case that the replicates across cohorts flag is set
    • it is not the case that the survives negative controls flag is set
    • the count modalities supporting is less than 2
    • the has pleiotropy flag is set
  2. false otherwise
In English

If not replicates across cohorts, not survives negative controls, count modalities supporting is less than 2, or has pleiotropy then true(), otherwise false().

As a formula
= IF(OR(NOT(Replicates Across Cohorts), NOT(Survives Negative Controls), Count Modalities Supporting < 2, Has Pleiotropy), TRUE(), FALSE())
Inputs 4 fields this rule reads
ƒReplicates Across CohortsCausalMechanisms If count replications is at least 2 and count concordant replications is at least 2 then true(), otherwise false(). ƒSurvives Negative ControlsCausalMechanisms If count neg control tests is at least 1 and count neg control survived equals count neg control tests then true(), otherwise false(). ΣCount Modalities SupportingCausalMechanisms Count of EvidenceItems whose causal mechanism is causal mechanism id, is cross modality is true(), and is qualified evidence is true(). ●Has PleiotropyCausalMechanisms True when mechanism shows pleiotropy across phenotypes.
Consumers 1 direct +32 transitive rules that read this one
ƒ Is Causal Architecture Node CausalMechanisms Count Confirmed Causal Nodes Individuals Sum Confirmed Causal Confidence Individuals Is Mechanism Matched Treatments Is Ancestry Transportable CausalMechanisms Individual Confirmed Node Count IndividualPredictions Individual Causal Mass IndividualPredictions Is Treatment Response Predicted Treatments Treatment Response Deciding Factor Treatments Count Cross Ancestry Confirmed Nodes Individuals Predicted Value IndividualPredictions Rests on Confirmed Mechanism IndividualPredictions Is Falsifiability Backed IndividualPredictions Count Predicted Treatment Responses Individuals Individual Cross Ancestry Node Count IndividualPredictions Patient Stratification Tier IndividualPredictions Is Clinically Actionable IndividualPredictions Lifecycle State Key IndividualPredictions Has Spurious Correlation Flag IndividualPredictions Is Severity Actionable IndividualPredictions Severity Deciding Factor IndividualPredictions Treatment Response Deciding Factor IndividualPredictions Deciding Gate IndividualPredictions Recommended Treatment Line IndividualPredictions Treatment Line Deciding Factor IndividualPredictions Has Predicted Treatment Response Individuals Is Transportable to Absent Ancestry IndividualPredictions Progression Vs Actionability Disagree IndividualPredictions Is High Confidence Prediction IndividualPredictions Individual Has Predicted Treatment Response IndividualPredictions Is Ancestry Transport Safe IndividualPredictions Transport Gate Status IndividualPredictions Is Treatment Response Actionable IndividualPredictions
Ground truth at the leaves 18 the raw values this ultimately depends on
● Causal Mechanism CohortReplications ● Causal Mechanism ID CausalMechanisms ● Replication P Value CohortReplications ● Replication Effect Sign CohortReplications ● Causal Mechanism NegativeControlTests ● Permutation Effect Size NegativeControlTests ● Null Threshold NegativeControlTests ● Causal Mechanism EvidenceItems ● Is Cross Modality EvidenceItems ● Measurement Error Score OmicsAssays ● Omics Assay EvidenceItems ● Omics Assay ID OmicsAssays ● Is Negative Control Arm EvidenceItems ● Standard Error EvidenceItems ● Effect Size EvidenceItems ● Is Adjusted for Ancestry P Cs EvidenceItems ● Is Adjusted for Batch EvidenceItems ● Has Pleiotropy CausalMechanisms

Derived: spurious unless replicated, survives controls, has >=2 modalities, and is not purely pleiotropic.

ƒ Causal Confidence Calculated 6 hops from ground truth number DR-124
In RuleSpeak

The causal mechanism's causal confidence is determined by the following priority:

  1. 1 if 0.30 times 1 if the count qualified evidence is at least 4, otherwise the count qualified evidence divided by 4 plus 0.20 times 1 if the count modalities supporting is at least 3, otherwise the count modalities supporting divided by 3 plus 0.30 times the replication fraction plus 0.20 times 1 if the survives negative controls flag is set, otherwise 0 is greater than 1
  2. 0.30 times 1 if the count qualified evidence is at least 4, otherwise the count qualified evidence divided by 4 plus 0.20 times 1 if the count modalities supporting is at least 3, otherwise the count modalities supporting divided by 3 plus 0.30 times the replication fraction plus 0.20 times 1 if the survives negative controls flag is set, otherwise 0 otherwise
In English

If ((((0.30 times if count qualified evidence is at least 4 then 1, otherwise (count qualified evidence divided by 4)) plus (0.20 times if count modalities supporting is at least 3 then 1, otherwise (count modalities supporting divided by 3))) plus (0.30 times replication fraction)) plus (0.20 times if survives negative controls then 1, otherwise 0)) is greater than 1 then 1, otherwise ((((0.30 times if count qualified evidence is at least 4 then 1, otherwise (count qualified evidence divided by 4)) plus (0.20 times if count modalities supporting is at least 3 then 1, otherwise (count modalities supporting divided by 3))) plus (0.30 times replication fraction)) plus (0.20 times if survives negative controls then 1, otherwise 0)).

As a formula
= IF(((0.30 * IF(Count Qualified Evidence >= 4, 1, Count Qualified Evidence / 4)) + (0.20 * IF(Count Modalities Supporting >= 3, 1, Count Modalities Supporting / 3)) + (0.30 * Replication Fraction) + (0.20 * IF(Survives Negative Controls, 1, 0))) > 1, 1, (0.30 * IF(Count Qualified Evidence >= 4, 1, Count Qualified Evidence / 4)) + (0.20 * IF(Count Modalities Supporting >= 3, 1, Count Modalities Supporting / 3)) + (0.30 * Replication Fraction) + (0.20 * IF(Survives Negative Controls, 1, 0)))
Inputs 4 fields this rule reads
ΣCount Qualified EvidenceCausalMechanisms Count of EvidenceItems whose causal mechanism is causal mechanism id and is qualified evidence is true(). ΣCount Modalities SupportingCausalMechanisms Count of EvidenceItems whose causal mechanism is causal mechanism id, is cross modality is true(), and is qualified evidence is true(). ƒReplication FractionCausalMechanisms If count replications is greater than 0 then (count concordant replications divided by count replications), otherwise 0. ƒSurvives Negative ControlsCausalMechanisms If count neg control tests is at least 1 and count neg control survived equals count neg control tests then true(), otherwise false().
Consumers 2 direct +31 transitive rules that read this one
Σ Sum Confirmed Causal Confidence Individuals ƒ Is Causal Architecture Node CausalMechanisms Individual Causal Mass IndividualPredictions Count Confirmed Causal Nodes Individuals Is Mechanism Matched Treatments Is Ancestry Transportable CausalMechanisms Predicted Value IndividualPredictions Individual Confirmed Node Count IndividualPredictions Is Treatment Response Predicted Treatments Treatment Response Deciding Factor Treatments Count Cross Ancestry Confirmed Nodes Individuals Patient Stratification Tier IndividualPredictions Is Clinically Actionable IndividualPredictions Lifecycle State Key IndividualPredictions Rests on Confirmed Mechanism IndividualPredictions Is Falsifiability Backed IndividualPredictions Count Predicted Treatment Responses Individuals Individual Cross Ancestry Node Count IndividualPredictions Deciding Gate IndividualPredictions Progression Vs Actionability Disagree IndividualPredictions Has Spurious Correlation Flag IndividualPredictions Is Severity Actionable IndividualPredictions Severity Deciding Factor IndividualPredictions Treatment Response Deciding Factor IndividualPredictions Recommended Treatment Line IndividualPredictions Treatment Line Deciding Factor IndividualPredictions Has Predicted Treatment Response Individuals Is Transportable to Absent Ancestry IndividualPredictions Is High Confidence Prediction IndividualPredictions Individual Has Predicted Treatment Response IndividualPredictions Is Ancestry Transport Safe IndividualPredictions Transport Gate Status IndividualPredictions Is Treatment Response Actionable IndividualPredictions
Ground truth at the leaves 17 the raw values this ultimately depends on
● Causal Mechanism EvidenceItems ● Causal Mechanism ID CausalMechanisms ● Measurement Error Score OmicsAssays ● Omics Assay EvidenceItems ● Omics Assay ID OmicsAssays ● Is Negative Control Arm EvidenceItems ● Standard Error EvidenceItems ● Effect Size EvidenceItems ● Is Adjusted for Ancestry P Cs EvidenceItems ● Is Adjusted for Batch EvidenceItems ● Is Cross Modality EvidenceItems ● Causal Mechanism CohortReplications ● Replication P Value CohortReplications ● Replication Effect Sign CohortReplications ● Causal Mechanism NegativeControlTests ● Permutation Effect Size NegativeControlTests ● Null Threshold NegativeControlTests

Derived bounded blend of qualified-evidence count, modality breadth, replication rate, and control survival.

↗ Variant is Causal Candidate Lookup 3 hops from ground truth boolean DR-125
In RuleSpeak

The causal mechanism's variant is causal candidate is determined by the following priority:

  1. false if the genomic variant is blank
  2. the is causal candidate of the causal mechanism's genomic variant otherwise
In English

If genomic variant is "" then false(), otherwise the is causal candidate of the linked genomic variant.

As a formula
= IF(Genomic Variant = "", FALSE(), INDEX(Is Causal Candidate, MATCH(Genomic Variant, Genomic Variant ID, 0)))
Inputs 3 fields this rule reads
●Genomic VariantCausalMechanisms Primary genomic variant if applicable. ƒIs Causal CandidateGenomicVariants If is rare variant or variant class is rare and has allele specific expression then true(), otherwise false(). ●Genomic Variant IDGenomicVariants Stable identifier for each genomic variant record.
Consumers 1 direct +32 transitive rules that read this one
ƒ Is Causal Architecture Node CausalMechanisms Count Confirmed Causal Nodes Individuals Sum Confirmed Causal Confidence Individuals Is Mechanism Matched Treatments Is Ancestry Transportable CausalMechanisms Individual Confirmed Node Count IndividualPredictions Individual Causal Mass IndividualPredictions Is Treatment Response Predicted Treatments Treatment Response Deciding Factor Treatments Count Cross Ancestry Confirmed Nodes Individuals Predicted Value IndividualPredictions Rests on Confirmed Mechanism IndividualPredictions Is Falsifiability Backed IndividualPredictions Count Predicted Treatment Responses Individuals Individual Cross Ancestry Node Count IndividualPredictions Patient Stratification Tier IndividualPredictions Is Clinically Actionable IndividualPredictions Lifecycle State Key IndividualPredictions Has Spurious Correlation Flag IndividualPredictions Is Severity Actionable IndividualPredictions Severity Deciding Factor IndividualPredictions Treatment Response Deciding Factor IndividualPredictions Deciding Gate IndividualPredictions Recommended Treatment Line IndividualPredictions Treatment Line Deciding Factor IndividualPredictions Has Predicted Treatment Response Individuals Is Transportable to Absent Ancestry IndividualPredictions Progression Vs Actionability Disagree IndividualPredictions Is High Confidence Prediction IndividualPredictions Individual Has Predicted Treatment Response IndividualPredictions Is Ancestry Transport Safe IndividualPredictions Transport Gate Status IndividualPredictions Is Treatment Response Actionable IndividualPredictions
Ground truth at the leaves 7 the raw values this ultimately depends on
● Genomic Variant CausalMechanisms ● Allele Frequency GenomicVariants ● Is Rare Variant Class VariantTypes ● Variant Type GenomicVariants ● Variant Type ID VariantTypes ● Has Allele Specific Expression GenomicVariants ● Genomic Variant ID GenomicVariants

Whether the linked variant is itself a derived causal candidate (empty-guarded).

ƒ Is Causal Architecture Node Calculated 7 hops from ground truth boolean DR-126
In RuleSpeak

The causal mechanism's is causal architecture node is determined by the following priority:

  1. true if all of these hold:
    • the causal confidence is at least 0.7
    • the is experimentally falsifiable flag is set
    • it is not the case that the is spurious derived flag is set
    • any of these hold:
      • the variant is causal candidate flag is set
      • the environmental exposure has a value
  2. false otherwise
In English

If causal confidence is at least 0.7, is experimentally falsifiable, not is spurious derived, and variant is causal candidate or environmental exposure is not "" then true(), otherwise false().

As a formula
= IF(AND(Causal Confidence >= 0.7, Is Experimentally Falsifiable, NOT(Is Spurious Derived), OR(Variant is Causal Candidate, Environmental Exposure <> "")), TRUE(), FALSE())
Inputs 5 fields this rule reads
ƒCausal ConfidenceCausalMechanisms If ((((0.30 times if count qualified evidence is at least 4 then 1, otherwise (count qualified evidence divided by 4)) plus (0.20 times if count modalities supporting is at least 3 then 1, otherwise (count modalities supporting divided by 3))) plus (0.30 times replication fraction)) plus (0.20 times if survives negative controls then 1, otherwise 0)) is greater than 1 then 1, otherwise ((((0.30 times if count qualified evidence is at least 4 then 1, otherwise (count qualified evidence divided by 4)) plus (0.20 times if count modalities supporting is at least 3 then 1, otherwise (count modalities supporting divided by 3))) plus (0.30 times replication fraction)) plus (0.20 times if survives negative controls then 1, otherwise 0)). ƒIs Experimentally FalsifiableCausalMechanisms If count intervention targets is at least 1 and count qualified evidence is at least 1 then true(), otherwise false(). ƒIs Spurious DerivedCausalMechanisms If not replicates across cohorts, not survives negative controls, count modalities supporting is less than 2, or has pleiotropy then true(), otherwise false(). ↗Variant is Causal CandidateCausalMechanisms If genomic variant is "" then false(), otherwise the is causal candidate of the linked genomic variant. ●Environmental ExposureCausalMechanisms Environmental exposure if gene-environment interaction.
Consumers 4 direct +28 transitive rules that read this one
Σ Count Confirmed Causal Nodes Individuals Σ Sum Confirmed Causal Confidence Individuals ↗ Is Mechanism Matched Treatments ƒ Is Ancestry Transportable CausalMechanisms Individual Confirmed Node Count IndividualPredictions Individual Causal Mass IndividualPredictions Is Treatment Response Predicted Treatments Treatment Response Deciding Factor Treatments Count Cross Ancestry Confirmed Nodes Individuals Predicted Value IndividualPredictions Rests on Confirmed Mechanism IndividualPredictions Is Falsifiability Backed IndividualPredictions Count Predicted Treatment Responses Individuals Individual Cross Ancestry Node Count IndividualPredictions Patient Stratification Tier IndividualPredictions Is Clinically Actionable IndividualPredictions Lifecycle State Key IndividualPredictions Has Spurious Correlation Flag IndividualPredictions Is Severity Actionable IndividualPredictions Severity Deciding Factor IndividualPredictions Treatment Response Deciding Factor IndividualPredictions Deciding Gate IndividualPredictions Recommended Treatment Line IndividualPredictions Treatment Line Deciding Factor IndividualPredictions Has Predicted Treatment Response Individuals Is Transportable to Absent Ancestry IndividualPredictions Progression Vs Actionability Disagree IndividualPredictions Is High Confidence Prediction IndividualPredictions Individual Has Predicted Treatment Response IndividualPredictions Is Ancestry Transport Safe IndividualPredictions Transport Gate Status IndividualPredictions Is Treatment Response Actionable IndividualPredictions

● Causal Mechanism EvidenceItems ● Causal Mechanism ID CausalMechanisms ● Measurement Error Score OmicsAssays ● Omics Assay EvidenceItems ● Omics Assay ID OmicsAssays ● Is Negative Control Arm EvidenceItems ● Standard Error EvidenceItems ● Effect Size EvidenceItems ● Is Adjusted for Ancestry P Cs EvidenceItems ● Is Adjusted for Batch EvidenceItems ● Is Cross Modality EvidenceItems ● Causal Mechanism CohortReplications ● Replication P Value CohortReplications ● Replication Effect Sign CohortReplications ● Causal Mechanism NegativeControlTests ● Permutation Effect Size NegativeControlTests ● Null Threshold NegativeControlTests ● Causal Mechanism InterventionTargets ● Has Pleiotropy CausalMechanisms ● Genomic Variant CausalMechanisms ● Allele Frequency GenomicVariants ● Is Rare Variant Class VariantTypes ● Variant Type GenomicVariants ● Variant Type ID VariantTypes ● Has Allele Specific Expression GenomicVariants ● Genomic Variant ID GenomicVariants ● Environmental Exposure CausalMechanisms

Derived: a confirmed causal edge - confident, falsifiable, non-spurious, and grounded in a candidate variant or a real exposure.

ƒ Is Ancestry Transportable Calculated 8 hops from ground truth boolean DR-127
In RuleSpeak

The causal mechanism's is ancestry transportable is determined by the following priority:

  1. true if all of these hold:
    • the is causal architecture node flag is set
    • the count cross ancestry concordant is at least 1
  2. false otherwise
In English

If is causal architecture node and count cross ancestry concordant is at least 1 then true(), otherwise false().

As a formula
= IF(AND(Is Causal Architecture Node, Count Cross Ancestry Concordant >= 1), TRUE(), FALSE())
Inputs 2 fields this rule reads
ƒIs Causal Architecture NodeCausalMechanisms If causal confidence is at least 0.7, is experimentally falsifiable, not is spurious derived, and variant is causal candidate or environmental exposure is not "" then true(), otherwise false(). ΣCount Cross Ancestry ConcordantCausalMechanisms Count of CohortReplications whose causal mechanism is causal mechanism id and is cross ancestry concordant is true().
Consumers 1 direct +8 transitive rules that read this one

Derived: a confirmed node whose effect replicated in >=1 different ancestry (measured invariance).

⇢ Intervention Targets Relationship string

A pointer to Intervention Target. It is not derived from anything else — it is the link the rest of the DAG hangs off.

Intervention targets derived from this mechanism.

⇢ Evidence Items Relationship string

A pointer to Evidence Item. It is not derived from anything else — it is the link the rest of the DAG hangs off.

Evidence items supporting this mechanism.

⇢ Cohort Replications Relationship string

A pointer to Cohort Replication. It is not derived from anything else — it is the link the rest of the DAG hangs off.

Cross-cohort replications of this mechanism.

⇢ Negative Control Tests Relationship string

A pointer to Negative Control Test. It is not derived from anything else — it is the link the rest of the DAG hangs off.

Negative-control tests on this mechanism.

Epistatic Interaction

Higher-order epistasis and pleiotropy interactions between genomic variants.

● Interaction Label Ground truth string

Ground truth. Written directly to the database (datatype string); not computed from anything else. Everything derived ultimately traces back to values like this one.

Human-readable interaction label.

● Individual Ground truth string

Ground truth. Written directly to the database (datatype string); not computed from anything else. Everything derived ultimately traces back to values like this one.

Individual exhibiting interaction.

● Primary Variant Ground truth string

Ground truth. Written directly to the database (datatype string); not computed from anything else. Everything derived ultimately traces back to values like this one.

First variant in interaction.

● Secondary Variant Ground truth string

Ground truth. Written directly to the database (datatype string); not computed from anything else. Everything derived ultimately traces back to values like this one.

Second variant in interaction.

● Epistasis Score Ground truth number

Ground truth. Written directly to the database (datatype number); not computed from anything else. Everything derived ultimately traces back to values like this one.

Higher-order epistasis effect score (0-1).

● Has Pleiotropy Ground truth boolean

Ground truth. Written directly to the database (datatype boolean); not computed from anything else. Everything derived ultimately traces back to values like this one.

True when interaction shows pleiotropy.

ƒ Name Calculated 1 hop from ground truth string DR-128
In RuleSpeak

Interaction label.

As a formula
= Interaction Label

ƒ Relative Path Calculated 4 hops from ground truth string DR-130

An epistatic interaction's relative path is computed as the parent path, followed by the literal “/epistasis/”, followed by the epistatic interaction ID.

(parent path joined with "/epistasis/") joined with epistatic interaction id.

As a formula
= Parent Path & "/epistasis/" & Epistatic Interaction ID

ƒ Is High Order Epistasis Calculated 1 hop from ground truth boolean DR-131
In RuleSpeak

The epistatic interaction's is high order epistasis is determined by the following priority:

  1. true if the epistasis score is greater than 0.5
  2. false otherwise
In English

If epistasis score is greater than 0.5 then true(), otherwise false().

As a formula
= IF(Epistasis Score > 0.5, TRUE(), FALSE())
Inputs 1 fields this rule reads
●Epistasis ScoreEpistaticInteractions Higher-order epistasis effect score (0-1).
Ground truth at the leaves 1 the raw values this ultimately depends on
● Epistasis Score EpistaticInteractions

True when epistasis score exceeds 0.5.

Ground truth. Written directly to the database (datatype string); not computed from anything else. Everything derived ultimately traces back to values like this one.

Ground truth. Written directly to the database (datatype string); not computed from anything else. Everything derived ultimately traces back to values like this one.

Hypothetical intervention (empty = no treatment).

↗ Individual Confirmed Node Count Lookup 9 hops from ground truth integer DR-143
In RuleSpeak

The individual prediction's individual confirmed node count is determined by the following priority:

  1. 0 if the individual is blank
  2. the count confirmed causal nodes of the individual prediction's individual otherwise
In English

If individual is "" then 0, otherwise the count confirmed causal nodes of the linked individual.

As a formula
= IF(Individual = "", 0, INDEX(Count Confirmed Causal Nodes, MATCH(Individual, Individual ID, 0)))
Inputs 3 fields this rule reads
●IndividualIndividualPredictions Individual predicted. ΣCount Confirmed Causal NodesIndividuals Count of CausalMechanisms whose individual is individual id and is causal architecture node is true(). ●Individual IDIndividuals Stable identifier for each individual in the million-person cohort.

In RuleSpeak

The individual prediction's individual has cryptic relatedness is determined by the following priority:

  1. false if the individual is blank
  2. the has cryptic relatedness flag of the individual prediction's individual otherwise
In English

If individual is "" then false(), otherwise the has cryptic relatedness flag of the linked individual.

As a formula
= IF(Individual = "", FALSE(), INDEX(Has Cryptic Relatedness Flag, MATCH(Individual, Individual ID, 0)))

  1. 0 if the individual is blank
  2. the max severity score of the individual prediction's individual otherwise

If individual is "" then false(), otherwise the has high severity phenotype of the linked individual.

If individual is "" then false(), otherwise the has predicted treatment response of the linked individual.

ƒ Predicted Value Calculated 10 hops from ground truth number DR-149
In RuleSpeak

The individual prediction's predicted value is determined by the following priority:

  1. 10 if 2 times the individual causal mass plus 1.5 times the individual confirmed node count is greater than 10
  2. 2 times the individual causal mass plus 1.5 times the individual confirmed node count otherwise
In English

If ((2 times individual causal mass) plus (1.5 times individual confirmed node count)) is greater than 10 then 10, otherwise ((2 times individual causal mass) plus (1.5 times individual confirmed node count)).

As a formula
= IF(((2 * Individual Causal Mass) + (1.5 * Individual Confirmed Node Count)) > 10, 10, (2 * Individual Causal Mass) + (1.5 * Individual Confirmed Node Count))
Inputs 2 fields this rule reads
↗Individual Causal MassIndividualPredictions If individual is "" then 0, otherwise the sum confirmed causal confidence of the linked individual. ↗Individual Confirmed Node CountIndividualPredictions If individual is "" then 0, otherwise the count confirmed causal nodes of the linked individual.
Consumers 3 direct +2 transitive rules that read this one
ƒ Patient Stratification Tier IndividualPredictions ƒ Is Clinically Actionable IndividualPredictions ƒ Lifecycle State Key IndividualPredictions Deciding Gate IndividualPredictions Progression Vs Actionability Disagree IndividualPredictions
Ground truth at the leaves 30 the raw values this ultimately depends on
● Individual IndividualPredictions ● Causal Mechanism EvidenceItems ● Causal Mechanism ID CausalMechanisms ● Measurement Error Score OmicsAssays ● Omics Assay EvidenceItems ● Omics Assay ID OmicsAssays ● Is Negative Control Arm EvidenceItems ● Standard Error EvidenceItems ● Effect Size EvidenceItems ● Is Adjusted for Ancestry P Cs EvidenceItems ● Is Adjusted for Batch EvidenceItems ● Is Cross Modality EvidenceItems ● Causal Mechanism CohortReplications ● Replication P Value CohortReplications ● Replication Effect Sign CohortReplications ● Causal Mechanism NegativeControlTests ● Permutation Effect Size NegativeControlTests ● Null Threshold NegativeControlTests ● Individual CausalMechanisms ● Individual ID Individuals ● Causal Mechanism InterventionTargets ● Has Pleiotropy CausalMechanisms ● Genomic Variant CausalMechanisms ● Allele Frequency GenomicVariants ● Is Rare Variant Class VariantTypes ● Variant Type GenomicVariants ● Variant Type ID VariantTypes ● Has Allele Specific Expression GenomicVariants ● Genomic Variant ID GenomicVariants ● Environmental Exposure CausalMechanisms

Derived risk magnitude (0-10), a monotone function of validated causal mass only - rides mechanism, not ancestry correlation.

Σ Count Bins Aggregation 1 hop from ground truth integer DR-150
In RuleSpeak

An individual prediction's count bins is the number of calibration bins related to the individual prediction.

In English

Count of CalibrationBins whose individual prediction is individual prediction id.

As a formula
= COUNTIFS(Individual Prediction, Individual Prediction ID)
Inputs 2 fields this rule reads
●Individual PredictionCalibrationBins FK to the prediction this bin calibrates. ●Individual Prediction IDIndividualPredictions Stable identifier for each prediction record.
Consumers 2 direct +6 transitive rules that read this one
ƒ Mean Bin Abs Error IndividualPredictions ƒ Well Calibrated Fraction IndividualPredictions Calibrated Uncertainty IndividualPredictions Is High Confidence Prediction IndividualPredictions Lifecycle State Key IndividualPredictions Deciding Gate IndividualPredictions Is Clinically Actionable IndividualPredictions Progression Vs Actionability Disagree IndividualPredictions
Ground truth at the leaves 2 the raw values this ultimately depends on
● Individual Prediction CalibrationBins ● Individual Prediction ID IndividualPredictions

Total reliability bins for this prediction.

Σ Count Well Calibrated Bins Aggregation 3 hops from ground truth integer DR-151
In RuleSpeak

An individual prediction's count well calibrated bins is the number of the individual prediction's calibration bins that are well calibrated bins.

In English

Count of CalibrationBins whose individual prediction is individual prediction id and is well calibrated bin is true().

As a formula
= COUNTIFS(Individual Prediction, Individual Prediction ID, Is Well Calibrated Bin, TRUE())
Inputs 3 fields this rule reads
●Individual PredictionCalibrationBins FK to the prediction this bin calibrates. ●Individual Prediction IDIndividualPredictions Stable identifier for each prediction record. ƒIs Well Calibrated BinCalibrationBins If coverage count is at least 20 and bin abs error is at most 0.1 then true(), otherwise false().
Consumers 1 direct +6 transitive rules that read this one
ƒ Well Calibrated Fraction IndividualPredictions Calibrated Uncertainty IndividualPredictions Is High Confidence Prediction IndividualPredictions Lifecycle State Key IndividualPredictions Deciding Gate IndividualPredictions Is Clinically Actionable IndividualPredictions Progression Vs Actionability Disagree IndividualPredictions
Ground truth at the leaves 5 the raw values this ultimately depends on
● Individual Prediction CalibrationBins ● Individual Prediction ID IndividualPredictions ● Coverage Count CalibrationBins ● Predicted Probability Band CalibrationBins ● Observed Event Rate CalibrationBins

Bins passing coverage and accuracy.

Σ Sum Bin Abs Error Aggregation 2 hops from ground truth number DR-152
In RuleSpeak

An individual prediction's sum bin abs error is the total bin abs error across the calibration bins related to the individual prediction.

In English

Sum of bin abs error across all CalibrationBins whose individual prediction is individual prediction id.

As a formula
= SUMIFS(Bin Abs Error, Individual Prediction, Individual Prediction ID)
Inputs 3 fields this rule reads
ƒBin Abs ErrorCalibrationBins If predicted probability band is at least observed event rate then (predicted probability band minus observed event rate), otherwise (observed event rate minus predicted probability band). ●Individual PredictionCalibrationBins FK to the prediction this bin calibrates. ●Individual Prediction IDIndividualPredictions Stable identifier for each prediction record.
Consumers 1 direct +6 transitive rules that read this one
ƒ Mean Bin Abs Error IndividualPredictions Calibrated Uncertainty IndividualPredictions Is High Confidence Prediction IndividualPredictions Lifecycle State Key IndividualPredictions Deciding Gate IndividualPredictions Is Clinically Actionable IndividualPredictions Progression Vs Actionability Disagree IndividualPredictions
Ground truth at the leaves 4 the raw values this ultimately depends on
● Predicted Probability Band CalibrationBins ● Observed Event Rate CalibrationBins ● Individual Prediction CalibrationBins ● Individual Prediction ID IndividualPredictions

Summed reliability gap across this prediction's bins.

ƒ Mean Bin Abs Error Calculated 3 hops from ground truth number DR-153
In RuleSpeak

The individual prediction's mean bin abs error is determined by the following priority:

  1. the sum bin abs error divided by the count bins if the count bins is greater than 0
  2. 1 otherwise
In English

If count bins is greater than 0 then (sum bin abs error divided by count bins), otherwise 1.

As a formula
= IF(Count Bins > 0, Sum Bin Abs Error / Count Bins, 1)
Inputs 2 fields this rule reads
ΣCount BinsIndividualPredictions Count of CalibrationBins whose individual prediction is individual prediction id. ΣSum Bin Abs ErrorIndividualPredictions Sum of bin abs error across all CalibrationBins whose individual prediction is individual prediction id.
Consumers 1 direct +5 transitive rules that read this one
ƒ Calibrated Uncertainty IndividualPredictions Is High Confidence Prediction IndividualPredictions Lifecycle State Key IndividualPredictions Deciding Gate IndividualPredictions Is Clinically Actionable IndividualPredictions Progression Vs Actionability Disagree IndividualPredictions
Ground truth at the leaves 4 the raw values this ultimately depends on
● Individual Prediction CalibrationBins ● Individual Prediction ID IndividualPredictions ● Predicted Probability Band CalibrationBins ● Observed Event Rate CalibrationBins

Derived mean reliability gap; defaults to 1 (worst) when no bins exist.

ƒ Well Calibrated Fraction Calculated 4 hops from ground truth number DR-154
In RuleSpeak

The individual prediction's well calibrated fraction is determined by the following priority:

  1. the count well calibrated bins divided by the count bins if the count bins is greater than 0
  2. 0 otherwise
In English

If count bins is greater than 0 then (count well calibrated bins divided by count bins), otherwise 0.

As a formula
= IF(Count Bins > 0, Count Well Calibrated Bins / Count Bins, 0)
Inputs 2 fields this rule reads
ΣCount BinsIndividualPredictions Count of CalibrationBins whose individual prediction is individual prediction id. ΣCount Well Calibrated BinsIndividualPredictions Count of CalibrationBins whose individual prediction is individual prediction id and is well calibrated bin is true().
Consumers 1 direct +5 transitive rules that read this one
ƒ Calibrated Uncertainty IndividualPredictions Is High Confidence Prediction IndividualPredictions Lifecycle State Key IndividualPredictions Deciding Gate IndividualPredictions Is Clinically Actionable IndividualPredictions Progression Vs Actionability Disagree IndividualPredictions
Ground truth at the leaves 5 the raw values this ultimately depends on
● Individual Prediction CalibrationBins ● Individual Prediction ID IndividualPredictions ● Coverage Count CalibrationBins ● Predicted Probability Band CalibrationBins ● Observed Event Rate CalibrationBins

Derived fraction of trustworthy bins (guarded division).

ƒ Calibrated Uncertainty Calculated 5 hops from ground truth number DR-155
In RuleSpeak

An individual prediction's calibrated uncertainty is computed as 0 if 1 minus the mean bin abs error is less than 0, otherwise 1 minus the mean bin abs error times the well calibrated fraction.

In English

If (1 minus mean bin abs error) is less than 0 then 0, otherwise (1 minus mean bin abs error) times well calibrated fraction.

As a formula
= IF((1 - Mean Bin Abs Error) < 0, 0, (1 - Mean Bin Abs Error)) * Well Calibrated Fraction
Inputs 2 fields this rule reads
ƒMean Bin Abs ErrorIndividualPredictions If count bins is greater than 0 then (sum bin abs error divided by count bins), otherwise 1. ƒWell Calibrated FractionIndividualPredictions If count bins is greater than 0 then (count well calibrated bins divided by count bins), otherwise 0.
Consumers 3 direct +2 transitive rules that read this one
ƒ Is High Confidence Prediction IndividualPredictions ƒ Lifecycle State Key IndividualPredictions ƒ Deciding Gate IndividualPredictions Is Clinically Actionable IndividualPredictions Progression Vs Actionability Disagree IndividualPredictions
Ground truth at the leaves 5 the raw values this ultimately depends on
● Individual Prediction CalibrationBins ● Individual Prediction ID IndividualPredictions ● Predicted Probability Band CalibrationBins ● Observed Event Rate CalibrationBins ● Coverage Count CalibrationBins

Derived reliability (HIGH = trustworthy): (1 - mean gap) scaled by well-covered-bin fraction; 0 for uncovered predictions.

ƒ Rests on Confirmed Mechanism Calculated 10 hops from ground truth boolean DR-156
In RuleSpeak

The individual prediction's rests on confirmed mechanism is determined by the following priority:

  1. true if the individual confirmed node count is at least 1
  2. false otherwise
In English

If individual confirmed node count is at least 1 then true(), otherwise false().

As a formula
= IF(Individual Confirmed Node Count >= 1, TRUE(), FALSE())
Inputs 1 fields this rule reads
↗Individual Confirmed Node CountIndividualPredictions If individual is "" then 0, otherwise the count confirmed causal nodes of the linked individual.

Ground truth at the leaves 30 the raw values this ultimately depends on
● Individual IndividualPredictions ● Individual CausalMechanisms ● Individual ID Individuals ● Causal Mechanism EvidenceItems ● Causal Mechanism ID CausalMechanisms ● Measurement Error Score OmicsAssays ● Omics Assay EvidenceItems ● Omics Assay ID OmicsAssays ● Is Negative Control Arm EvidenceItems ● Standard Error EvidenceItems ● Effect Size EvidenceItems ● Is Adjusted for Ancestry P Cs EvidenceItems ● Is Adjusted for Batch EvidenceItems ● Is Cross Modality EvidenceItems ● Causal Mechanism CohortReplications ● Replication P Value CohortReplications ● Replication Effect Sign CohortReplications ● Causal Mechanism NegativeControlTests ● Permutation Effect Size NegativeControlTests ● Null Threshold NegativeControlTests ● Causal Mechanism InterventionTargets ● Has Pleiotropy CausalMechanisms ● Genomic Variant CausalMechanisms ● Allele Frequency GenomicVariants ● Is Rare Variant Class VariantTypes ● Variant Type GenomicVariants ● Variant Type ID VariantTypes ● Has Allele Specific Expression GenomicVariants ● Genomic Variant ID GenomicVariants ● Environmental Exposure CausalMechanisms

Derived: grounded in >=1 validated mechanism.

ƒ Has Spurious Correlation Flag Calculated 11 hops from ground truth boolean DR-157
In RuleSpeak

The individual prediction's has spurious correlation flag is determined by the following priority:

  1. true if any of these hold:
    • it is not the case that the rests on confirmed mechanism flag is set
    • the individual has cryptic relatedness flag is set
  2. false otherwise
In English

If not rests on confirmed mechanism or individual has cryptic relatedness then true(), otherwise false().

As a formula
= IF(OR(NOT(Rests on Confirmed Mechanism), Individual Has Cryptic Relatedness), TRUE(), FALSE())
Inputs 2 fields this rule reads
ƒRests on Confirmed MechanismIndividualPredictions If individual confirmed node count is at least 1 then true(), otherwise false(). ↗Individual Has Cryptic RelatednessIndividualPredictions If individual is "" then false(), otherwise the has cryptic relatedness flag of the linked individual.

Derived: spurious if no validated mechanism OR cryptic-relatedness leakage.

ƒ Is Falsifiability Backed Calculated 10 hops from ground truth boolean DR-158
In RuleSpeak

The individual prediction's is falsifiability backed is determined by the following priority:

  1. true if the individual confirmed node count is at least 1
  2. false otherwise
In English

If individual confirmed node count is at least 1 then true(), otherwise false().

As a formula
= IF(Individual Confirmed Node Count >= 1, TRUE(), FALSE())
Inputs 1 fields this rule reads
↗Individual Confirmed Node CountIndividualPredictions If individual is "" then 0, otherwise the count confirmed causal nodes of the linked individual.

● Individual IndividualPredictions ● Individual CausalMechanisms ● Individual ID Individuals ● Causal Mechanism EvidenceItems ● Causal Mechanism ID CausalMechanisms ● Measurement Error Score OmicsAssays ● Omics Assay EvidenceItems ● Omics Assay ID OmicsAssays ● Is Negative Control Arm EvidenceItems ● Standard Error EvidenceItems ● Effect Size EvidenceItems ● Is Adjusted for Ancestry P Cs EvidenceItems ● Is Adjusted for Batch EvidenceItems ● Is Cross Modality EvidenceItems ● Causal Mechanism CohortReplications ● Replication P Value CohortReplications ● Replication Effect Sign CohortReplications ● Causal Mechanism NegativeControlTests ● Permutation Effect Size NegativeControlTests ● Null Threshold NegativeControlTests ● Causal Mechanism InterventionTargets ● Has Pleiotropy CausalMechanisms ● Genomic Variant CausalMechanisms ● Allele Frequency GenomicVariants ● Is Rare Variant Class VariantTypes ● Variant Type GenomicVariants ● Variant Type ID VariantTypes ● Has Allele Specific Expression GenomicVariants ● Genomic Variant ID GenomicVariants ● Environmental Exposure CausalMechanisms

Derived: inherits falsifiability - every confirmed node required IsExperimentallyFalsifiable.

ƒ Is Transportable to Absent Ancestry Calculated 12 hops from ground truth boolean DR-159
In RuleSpeak

The individual prediction's is transportable to absent ancestry is determined by the following priority:

  1. true if all of these hold:
    • the is ancestry holdout flag is set
    • the individual cross ancestry node count is at least 1
    • it is not the case that the has spurious correlation flag is set
  2. false otherwise
In English

If is ancestry holdout, individual cross ancestry node count is at least 1, and not has spurious correlation flag then true(), otherwise false().

As a formula
= IF(AND(Is Ancestry Holdout, Individual Cross Ancestry Node Count >= 1, NOT(Has Spurious Correlation Flag)), TRUE(), FALSE())
Inputs 3 fields this rule reads
ƒIs Ancestry HoldoutIndividualPredictions The is ancestry absent from training of the linked individual. ↗Individual Cross Ancestry Node CountIndividualPredictions If individual is "" then 0, otherwise the count cross ancestry confirmed nodes of the linked individual. ƒHas Spurious Correlation FlagIndividualPredictions If not rests on confirmed mechanism or individual has cryptic relatedness then true(), otherwise false().
Consumers 2 direct +4 transitive rules that read this one
ƒ Is Ancestry Transport Safe IndividualPredictions ƒ Transport Gate Status IndividualPredictions Is Clinically Actionable IndividualPredictions Lifecycle State Key IndividualPredictions Deciding Gate IndividualPredictions Progression Vs Actionability Disagree IndividualPredictions
Ground truth at the leaves 34 the raw values this ultimately depends on
● Is Ancestry Absent From Training Individuals ● Individual IndividualPredictions ● Individual ID Individuals ● Individual CausalMechanisms ● Causal Mechanism EvidenceItems ● Causal Mechanism ID CausalMechanisms ● Measurement Error Score OmicsAssays ● Omics Assay EvidenceItems ● Omics Assay ID OmicsAssays ● Is Negative Control Arm EvidenceItems ● Standard Error EvidenceItems ● Effect Size EvidenceItems ● Is Adjusted for Ancestry P Cs EvidenceItems ● Is Adjusted for Batch EvidenceItems ● Is Cross Modality EvidenceItems ● Causal Mechanism CohortReplications ● Replication P Value CohortReplications ● Replication Effect Sign CohortReplications ● Causal Mechanism NegativeControlTests ● Permutation Effect Size NegativeControlTests ● Null Threshold NegativeControlTests ● Causal Mechanism InterventionTargets ● Has Pleiotropy CausalMechanisms ● Genomic Variant CausalMechanisms ● Allele Frequency GenomicVariants ● Is Rare Variant Class VariantTypes ● Variant Type GenomicVariants ● Variant Type ID VariantTypes ● Has Allele Specific Expression GenomicVariants ● Genomic Variant ID GenomicVariants ● Environmental Exposure CausalMechanisms ● Replication Ancestry Label CohortReplications ● Ancestry Label Individuals ● Has Cryptic Relatedness Flag Individuals

Derived: a holdout individual is transportable only with >=1 cross-ancestry-replicated node and no spurious flag.

ƒ Is Ancestry Transport Safe Calculated 13 hops from ground truth boolean DR-160
In RuleSpeak

The individual prediction's is ancestry transport safe is determined by the following priority:

  1. the is transportable to absent ancestry if the is ancestry holdout flag is set
  2. true otherwise
In English

If is ancestry holdout then is transportable to absent ancestry, otherwise true().

As a formula
= IF(Is Ancestry Holdout, Is Transportable to Absent Ancestry, TRUE())
Inputs 2 fields this rule reads
ƒIs Ancestry HoldoutIndividualPredictions The is ancestry absent from training of the linked individual. ƒIs Transportable to Absent AncestryIndividualPredictions If is ancestry holdout, individual cross ancestry node count is at least 1, and not has spurious correlation flag then true(), otherwise false().
Consumers 3 direct +1 transitive rules that read this one
ƒ Is Clinically Actionable IndividualPredictions ƒ Lifecycle State Key IndividualPredictions ƒ Deciding Gate IndividualPredictions Progression Vs Actionability Disagree IndividualPredictions
